## Sixth Ingestion Canon — 2026-03-18

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Faction asymmetry model | SETTLED | Hybrid asymmetry. All nine houses share the full tech tree and unit roster. Each house has subtle base stat variations on shared units (Off/Def starting ratings) and one or more unique units accessible only to that house. Differences must be subtle — no house is dominant at match start. Unique unit assignments are a design gap pending full house-by-house mapping. At minimum: one house (axe-iconography, likely Ironmark) has access to a unique Axeman unit; at least one house has a base Pikeman with a defensively stronger starting stat. | 2026-03-18 |
| Base unit Off/Def stat system | SETTLED | All base units have an offensive and defensive rating. The neutral baseline is Off 5 / Def 5. House asymmetry applies small per-house deviations to shared units: examples include 5/6 (more defensive), 6/5 (more offensive), 4/6 (weaker offense, stronger defense). Deviations are intentionally subtle to prevent early-game dominance. Full stat assignments per house pending. | 2026-03-18 |
| Map structure | SETTLED | All three map structures layered. Massive continuous terrain (RTS movement, Warcraft 3-style direct unit control) forms the base experience. Province regions overlay the terrain and define territory/loyalty zones for governance, conviction, and diplomatic systems. Underlying tile/hex structure handles territory boundary calculations. Map scale is roughly ten times a typical RTS map with no upper ceiling imposed. | 2026-03-18 |
| Bloodline member aging and death | SETTLED | Aging out is very rare under normal conditions. Bloodline members die primarily through combat, assassination, illness events, sacrifice, and other in-game actions — not automatically from time. Exception: the initial starting leader options (Father, Uncle, or equivalent) who were born before or during The Great Frost are genuinely old at match start. These figures will die within the match timeframe. Their age is a feature, not a flaw — it makes the starting choice meaningful and their death narratively significant. No other bloodline members age to natural death except through extraordinary circumstances. | 2026-03-18 |
| Faith victory — Divine Right declaration mechanic | SETTLED | Faith victory is declared, not passively accumulated. The dynasty must formally declare Divine Right — their bloodline as blood descendants of the covenant's highest power. The declaration spreads across the world over a defined time window before victory can be claimed. All milestone requirements and the declaration clock are visible to all players at all times from the moment of declaration. Rival dynasties may form coalitions and mount a military challenge during the spread window. If the dynasty survives and faith thresholds are maintained, the victory is recognized. If the coalition defeats the dynasty before the window closes, the claim fails. Pre-declaration requirements (faith intensity, global faith share, specific structures) are also publicly known in advance so opposition can begin before a declaration is ever made. | 2026-03-18 |
| Divine Right milestone transparency | SETTLED | All milestone progression toward and during a Divine Right claim is fully visible to all players. Opacity is rejected. The drama is the race, not the surprise. This applies both to pre-declaration threshold requirements and to the spread clock after declaration. | 2026-03-18 |
| Trueborn City as world knowledge hub | SETTLED | The Trueborn City is the primary conduit for global information. Trade routes passing through the city carry news from across the world — population sentiment, faith distribution, political events, and faction movements. This is how the player observes global faith tracking data and world population conviction posture. Dynasties with active trade relationships with the city receive accurate, timely world-state information. Dynasties cut off from trade (blockaded, diplomatically isolated, or at war with the city's trade partners) see degraded or delayed information. Conquering the city grants information dominance in addition to economic and diplomatic advantages. | 2026-03-18 |

---

## Seventh Ingestion Canon — 2026-03-19

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Frost Elder system | ABANDONED | The Frost Elder mechanic (guaranteed-death starting leader who lived through The Great Frost) is abandoned. Prior SETTLED decision in Sixth Ingestion is superseded. All creative branch content referencing Frost Elder is void. Starting leader options (Father, Uncle, or equivalent) remain as a concept but do not carry mandatory death mechanics. | 2026-03-19 |
| Bloodline succession — player choice | SETTLED | When the primary bloodline member (head of bloodline) dies, the player chooses their successor. The succession interface shows each eligible candidate's attributes with visible projected impact on the bloodline as a whole — military standing, economic output, conviction trajectory, faith alignment, population loyalty. The choice is consequential. Some successor selections cause uprising events — lesser houses or territories that were loyal to the previous head revolt and must be reconquered or reclaimed. | 2026-03-19 |
| Bloodline family depth | SETTLED | Marriages, children, and generational offspring are a SIGNIFICANT core mechanic. The bloodline is a living family across multiple generations. Family management — who marries whom, how many children, political marriages to other bloodlines or lesser houses, which children are committed to which roles — is deep and central to the game identity. Managing a large family with many members is the intended experience. The upper bound of scope for this system is not defined. | 2026-03-19 |
| Bloodline member functional roles | SETTLED | Bloodline members have functional commitment roles that determine how they contribute. Members do not need to physically occupy buildings or spaces. Confirmed role categories: War commitment (member functions as general in battle, provides combat buffs/command bonuses to armies), Trade/Economy commitment (member can travel with or anchor a caravan, provides economic bonuses), Defense commitment (member stands as anchor for a specific claimed territory or region, provides loyalty and stability bonuses). Additional role categories are a design gap pending full expansion. | 2026-03-19 |
| Keep/home bloodline visualization | SETTLED | The Keep/home UI shows the primary bloodline family as visible, present characters — head of bloodline, spouse(s), children, key household members. Their status, faith alignment, and conviction are visible through their appearance and the state of the keep itself. This is a visual-first representation of the dynasty's health, not a text-driven interface. As members die, are born, or change status, the Keep reflects it. | 2026-03-19 |
| Victory difficulty standard | SETTLED | All victory paths are VERY difficult. Extended match timetable applies across the board. No path should feel achievable without significant dedication and follow-through. Individual dynasty victory over all others is VERY VERY VERY difficult — designed to be rare. The game naturally trends toward stalemate, coalition formation, and political resolution rather than clean solo conquest. This difficulty standard is a non-negotiable design commitment. | 2026-03-19 |
| Alliance Victory path | SETTLED | A new victory path: two or more bloodlines agree to terms of shared rulership with one ultimate ruler agreed upon by the coalition. This is not a soft alliance — it requires binding agreement on the terms of who rules. The population must accept the resolution: the match tracks what percentage of the world population has accepted the coalition's authority. Revolt continues until X% of population recognizes the agreed ruler. Achieving Alliance Victory is VERY VERY VERY difficult — coalition partners have competing interests, populations revolt, and the terms of rulership are contested throughout. This is a designed outlet for matches where individual dominance is impossible. | 2026-03-19 |
| The sovereignty seat — end goal | SETTLED | The bloodlines are fighting to become THE primary ruler of all lands — one bloodline (or coalition with an agreed head) recognized as the supreme authority. This is the Iron Throne equivalent concept (not named that in-game). Every victory path is an alternative route to this same claim. No bloodline wins by being second. | 2026-03-19 |
| Trueborn City — active rise arc | SETTLED | If no bloodline has conquered or claimed the Trueborn City by a defined threshold point in the match, the Trueborn City activates. The Trueborn — who once ruled all lands before The Great Frost — raise their banners and begin actively asserting themselves as the legitimate authority. They become a major NPC faction that acts against all bloodlines not significantly aligned with them. They pursue their own reconquest of the land. This creates a forced pressure mechanic: the city cannot be ignored indefinitely. A bloodline that conquers the city demonstrates the capacity to rule all others. A bloodline that lets the city rise must contend with a powerful, historically legitimate rival. | 2026-03-19 |
| Trueborn City — conquering the seat | SETTLED | Conquering the Trueborn City is not a victory condition. It is a strategic objective that provides significant bonuses to the conquering bloodline's pursuit of whichever victory condition they are pursuing. Holding the city accelerates all paths but does not itself constitute a win. If no bloodline conquers it by the activation threshold, the city becomes an active NPC adversary. | 2026-03-19 |
| Territorial governance difficulty | SETTLED | The Territorial Governance victory path requires development of ALL owned lands, including all tribal territories under control. It is near-impossible — a dynasty pursuing this path faces constant revolts, loyalty crises, and population resistance. It is not literally impossible but represents the highest difficulty path. A dynasty that achieves it has governed every corner of the land into willing acceptance. | 2026-03-19 |
| Faith doctrine tone standard | SETTLED | Faith doctrine mechanics, rituals, and events are serious, adult, and R-rated in design sensibility. This is not a game for children. Ritual design must reflect genuine cost, darkness, and consequence. Anything described as "vigil-keeping," "candle lighting," or similarly sanitized is explicitly rejected. The four covenants are treated as genuinely different and serious worldviews with real weight. | 2026-03-19 |
| Faith doctrine — full rewrite required | SETTLED | The existing faith doctrine design (Creative Branch 002) is superseded. A complete rewrite from the adult/serious tone standard is required. All four covenants and both doctrine paths must be redesigned under this standard. | 2026-03-19 |
| Bloodline population/family scope | OPEN | The mechanics for managing a very large number of living family members across generations — birth, childhood, maturity, death, marriage, and offspring across potentially hundreds of members — are an open design question. A system for managing generational scale (potentially using environmental events like long winters or famine as natural population thinning mechanics) has been considered but not designed. This is a high-priority open design gap. | 2026-03-19 |

---

## Eighth Ingestion Canon — 2026-03-19

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Tonal anchor — world feel | SETTLED | The world of Bloodlines must feel like a living, breathing version of the world behind Game of Thrones. Major houses in political conflict. Named heroes with personal stakes. Betrayal, marriage alliances, succession crises, assassination, political maneuvering — all as consequential as military warfare. The world has history, grudges, and ambition. No house is purely good or evil. Every powerful character is complex. The match should feel like it is generating a story, not just a game outcome. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| House historical rivalries | SETTLED | Named rivalries between specific founding houses must be designed. Some houses have historical conflicts, territorial disputes, or ideological incompatibilities that predate the match. Others have natural alliances. These starting relationships are not hard-locks — they can be changed in play — but they define the political landscape at match start and create natural story beats. Full rivalry and alliance mapping is a design gap pending completion. | 2026-03-19 |
| Bloodline Member personality traits | SETTLED | Beyond functional role, each Bloodline Member has personality traits that affect how they interact with the world — how they make decisions in events, how NPCs respond to them, what choices they push the player toward. A Cruel Warlord and a Moral Warlord fill the same role but feel completely different to play with and against. Trait design is a design gap pending completion. | 2026-03-19 |
| Heroic identity of Bloodline Members | SETTLED | Bloodline Members are the heroes of Bloodlines. They must feel like named characters with specific identities, not stat blocks. The player should mourn when a beloved member dies. Exceptional members should become legendary — known across the world by reputation. The narrative weight of who lives and dies IS the game's emotional core. | 2026-03-19 |
| Political conflict as equal to military conflict | SETTLED | Political maneuvering — marriages, succession crises, betrayal, assassination, diplomatic manipulation — carries equal weight to military action as a strategic tool. A house that wins through politics is as legitimate a winner as one that wins through armies. The design must reflect this parity at every level: events, mechanics, victory paths, and player tools. | 2026-03-19 |
| Narrative generation as a design goal | SETTLED | Every match should generate a story worth retelling. The systems — political events, bloodline deaths, succession crises, betrayals, faith conversions, coalition formations — must produce narrative moments that feel authored even though they are emergent. A player should finish a match and want to describe what happened as a story, not a score summary. | 2026-03-19 |
| House lore depth | SETTLED | Each of the nine founding houses must have designed lore: their history before The Great Frost, what they lost, what they believe about the world, and why they fight. Not flavor text — foundational identity that explains their mechanical tendencies and their rivalries. House lore design is a gap pending completion. | 2026-03-19 |
| House starting relationships | SETTLED | Seven canonical starting relationships at match start, canonicalized 2026-04-07: Goldgrave vs Whitehall (economic rivalry); Ironmark vs Stonehelm (military respect/wariness); Highborne vs Trueborn (prestige competition); Hartvale + Stonehelm (natural partnership: food for defense); Oldcrest vs Westland (cultural friction: conservatism vs frontier impulse); Trueborn vs Blood Dominion dynasties (ideological tension); Whitehall with all houses (universal diplomatic access by design). These are tendencies, not locks. Player action can alter them. | 2026-04-07 |

---

*New canonical decisions are appended to the relevant table as they are made. Rows are never deleted, only updated with new status and date.*

---

## Ninth Ingestion Canon — Ironmark Bloodline Lock — 2026-03-19

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Ironmark identity statement | SETTLED | "Ironmark is the Bloodline that reaches power first and pays for it in blood." This is a design specification, not flavor. Every Ironmark mechanic must reinforce the duality of accelerated power acquisition paired with compounding human cost. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark primary color | SETTLED | Charcoal Iron — NOT pure black. Charcoal Iron is the real color of worked iron, forge-smoke, and soot-stained stone. Prior "Black" hair/color assignment remains as hair color designation but the full visual identity uses Charcoal Iron as primary. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark accent color | SETTLED | Ember Red — used sparingly and with intention only. Represents forge heat, blood cost, consequence. Its rarity is its meaning: Ember Red marks veteran units, sacrifice markers, critical structures, escalation moments. Never used as a background or fill color. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark dominant symbol | SETTLED | The Axe — specifically as a tool of industry that became a weapon of war, not as a purely martial symbol. Five controlled variants: War Axe (military), Split Axe (political/diplomatic), Forging Axe (economic/infrastructure), Broken Axe (memorial), Raised Axe (active military commitment). Symbol variation must match context. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark societal advantage | SETTLED | Early iron access — Ironmark reaches iron infrastructure 20-30% faster than standard progression. Creates a meaningful early-to-mid game power window before other bloodlines catch the iron curve. Translates to early Axeman access, iron-reinforced fortifications, cavalry equipment ahead of schedule. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark required disadvantage | SETTLED | Blood Production — three components: (1) Increased combat casualties due to aggressive forge-culture fighting posture; (2) Reinforcement inefficiency from forge labor/military labor competition; (3) Population strain under sustained operations from compounding effect of (1) and (2). Short decisive wars are manageable. Long attrition works against Ironmark even when winning. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Global squad model | SETTLED | One selection = one squad = five individual unit-characters. Shared health pool with visual degradation — individual members fall as the squad takes damage, formation tightens visually as losses mount. Five remaining = full strength; one remaining = single survivor fighting alone. Applies to all bloodlines. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark squad trait — Ferocity Under Loss | SETTLED | As Ironmark squad size decreases from five toward one, remaining members gain progressive combat bonuses: increased attack speed, reduced routing tendency, fear-aura suppressing enemy morale. Does not eliminate population cost — dead members are gone regardless. In-battle, Ironmark losses make survivors fight harder. Axeman ferocity bonus is the most pronounced. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Ironmark unit values — locked | SETTLED | Swordsman: 5/5 (locked at neutral, superseding prior CB002 exploration of 6/5). Militia: 6/5. Spearmen/Hunters/Bowmen: 5/5. Axeman exclusive: Off 6 / Def 4. The Swordsman is baseline; asymmetry comes from the Axeman. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |
| Axeman unit specification | SETTLED | Ironmark-exclusive heavy infantry. Off 6 / Def 4. Exceptional against armored units and structures (bonus damage to fortifications, siege equipment, heavy infantry). Weak against fast units (cavalry, skirmishers). Ferocity Under Loss bonus most pronounced on this unit type. LOCKED. | 2026-03-19 |

---

## Tenth Session Canon — Operations System — 2026-03-21

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Operations system architecture | PROPOSED | Three categories: Covert Operations (rogues), Faith Operations (mystics), Military Operations (army doctrines). Core loop: Commit → Navigate → Execute → Consequence. All operations are real-time tactical execution, not abstract percentage rolls. | 2026-03-21 |
| Covert operations roster | PROPOSED | 10 operations: Intelligence (Scout Defenses, Infiltrate Court, Map Network, Identify Bloodline), Sabotage (Arson, Poison Supply, Sabotage Infrastructure), Direct Action (Assassinate, Kidnap, Incite Revolt). Executed by Rogue-class units (squad size 3, Off 3/Def 2) or Spymaster bloodline members. | 2026-03-21 |
| Faith operations roster | PROPOSED | 10 operations per covenant (40 total): 3 offensive, 3 defensive/utility, 2 light doctrine-specific, 2 dark doctrine-specific. Executed by covenant-specific Mystic units (squad size 1, Off 2/Def 3). Operations consume faith intensity as fuel. | 2026-03-21 |
| Military operations roster | PROPOSED | 6 attack doctrines: Conquer, Plunder, Pillage, Raze, Siege, Massacre. Chosen before engagement. Defenders do not know doctrine until behavior becomes apparent. Massacre hard-locked for Moral conviction dynasties (army refuses). | 2026-03-21 |
| Rogue unit type | PROPOSED | Level 2+ unit, squad size 3, Off 3/Def 2, fastest non-cavalry infantry, stealth capability, requires Spymaster network. | 2026-03-21 |
| Mystic unit type | PROPOSED | Level 3+ faith unit, squad size 1, Off 2/Def 3, covenant-specific, personal faith intensity pool that depletes with operations and recharges near faith buildings. | 2026-03-21 |
| Day/night cycle | PROPOSED | ~20 minutes real-time per full cycle: Dawn (~2 min), Day (~8 min), Dusk (~2 min), Night (~8 min). 6-hour match = ~18 in-game days. | 2026-03-21 |
| Lunar cycle | PROPOSED | Four phases rotating every 4-5 in-game days: New Moon, Waxing Moon, Full Moon, Waning Moon. Each affects different operation categories and covenant effectiveness. | 2026-03-21 |
| Seasonal cycle | PROPOSED | Four seasons rotating every 4-5 in-game days: Spring, Summer, Autumn, Winter. Affects economic output, military mobility, and faith/covert operation effectiveness. | 2026-03-21 |
| Celestial events | PROPOSED | Rare events 2-3 per match: Solar Eclipse, Blood Moon, Solstice (Summer/Winter), Comet Sighting. Visible in advance to all players. Dramatic effects on faith operations and match dynamics. | 2026-03-21 |
| Timing as universal strategic layer | PROPOSED | Time of day, moon phase, season, and world events are universal modifiers across all three operation categories. The *when* matters as much as the *what* and *where*. | 2026-03-21 |
| Dishonorable timing combinations | PROPOSED | Cruel conviction dynasties gain access to devastating multi-operation sequences with compounding conviction costs. Night Burning + Dawn Assault, Poison Supply + Winter Siege, Massacre + Feral Terror, Incite Revolt + Conquer. | 2026-03-21 |
| Massacre moral hard-lock | PROPOSED | A Moral conviction dynasty's army physically refuses to execute Massacre. This is the only hard-lock in the operations system. All other operations available to all conviction bands with scaling consequences. | 2026-03-21 |
| Faith switching permitted | PROPOSED | Players can switch covenants after initial selection at great cost: faith intensity reset to zero, population disruption, bloodline member faith crisis, diplomatic consequences, loss of all faith-specific military units until new thresholds reached. Designed as genuine last resort, not casual optimization. | 2026-03-21 |
| Utopia balance philosophy | PROPOSED | House asymmetry follows Utopia's balance model: mandatory disadvantages for every advantage, percentage-based modifiers (+10% to +50%), multiple independent power axes, soft counters not hard counters, penalties define identity as much as bonuses. | 2026-03-21 |

---

## Eleventh Session Canon — Dynastic Feedback and Corrections — 2026-03-26

Source: SESSION_2026-03-26_dynastic-feedback-and-corrections.md

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Marriage player control | SETTLED | Marriage does not happen naturally in Bloodlines. It is highly controlled and determined by the head of the household/player. No automatic matchmaking, no background marriage events. Every marriage is a deliberate player decision. | 2026-03-26 |
| Hybrid heir loyalty control | SETTLED | Children of cross-dynasty marriages CANNOT freely declare loyalty to either bloodline or form a new dynasty. All branching and loyalty declarations are controlled by the head of the household/player. Branching can be PROPOSED in-game with advantages/disadvantages presented, but is ultimately determined by the player. Corrects earlier text that implied children could freely choose. | 2026-03-26 |
| Polygamy faith restriction | SETTLED | Polygamy is permitted only under Blood Dominion and The Wild. In other faiths (Old Light, The Order), members can be withheld from marriage until a suitable enslavement or capture provides a marriage opportunity. | 2026-03-26 |
| Mixed bloodline defection slider | SETTLED | A slider tracks the intentions of children and members regarding defection: Loyal to head of bloodline (vassal), Neutral (NPC behavior), Seeking to join a different bloodline entirely. Head of family decides response, which may include forced combat. Reverse defection (others seeking to join your bloodline) requires successful diplomatic action first. | 2026-03-26 |
| Mixed bloodline notification system | SETTLED | The game provides ample notification about the status of bloodline members or children born seeking to branch off. The head of the bloodline makes the final decision. No silent defection. | 2026-03-26 |
| Training path — Mysticism | SETTLED | Mysticism added as seventh training path. Less focused on expanding and increasing faith, more focused on the offensive/defensive abilities of faith in war. Distinct from Religious Leadership path which handles institutional faith growth. | 2026-03-26 |
| Training paths complete list | SETTLED | Seven training paths confirmed: Military Command, Governance, Religious Leadership, Diplomacy, Covert Operations, Economic Stewardship/Trade, Mysticism. | 2026-03-26 |
| Bloodline member roles update | SETTLED | Confirmed roles: Commanders, Governors, Diplomats, Ideological Leader (Priest/Priestess), Merchant, Sorcerer. Sorcerer is the functional deployment of Mysticism-trained members. | 2026-03-26 |
| War hero promotion pipeline | SETTLED | Combat units not affiliated with the bloodline earn promotions through battle across 5 stages. Beyond stage 5, may be offered command promotion (general/commander role). After multiple successful campaigns, may be offered lands and titles to become a lesser house affiliated with the dynasty. Promoted units gain cumulative combat advantages at each stage. | 2026-03-26 |
| Promoted unit combat advantage | SETTLED | An army of promoted swordsmen would likely beat an army of regular class axemen despite base unit-type advantages, establishing that combat experience has real mechanical weight that can overcome type matchups. | 2026-03-26 |
| Early game NPC tribe focus | SETTLED | A large part of the early game should focus on battling or taking over NPC tribes. Conflict between founding houses should be extremely minimal in the early stages. The early game is about local consolidation, not inter-house warfare. | 2026-03-26 |
| Anti-cheese design principle | SETTLED | Blitz strategies, mass buildups that abandon non-military systems, and other cheese tactics should be discouraged by game design. Every viable strategy requires investment across multiple systems (dynasty, economy, faith, population). No shortcut bypasses the interconnected system requirements. | 2026-03-26 |
| Starting leader age ranges | SETTLED | Father: 30-40 years old. Eldest Son: 10-20. Second Eldest: less than 10. Brother of the King (uncle): 25-35 (slightly younger than father). Brother of the King's first eldest: 12-16. | 2026-03-26 |

---

## Twelfth Session Canon — Lance Design Decisions — 2026-04-07

Source: Live session decisions from Lance Fisher, 2026-04-07.

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Hartvale unique unit | SETTLED | Verdant Warden (Option A). Off 4 / Def 5. Hybrid guardian unit — can fight but is primarily a defender. Provides settlement defense bonuses and population loyalty bonuses. Represents Hartvale as protectors of their people. CB002 design confirmed over CB004 Hearthmasters. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Dynastic Prestige as victory path | ABANDONED | Dynastic Prestige is no longer a standalone victory path. Accumulating prestige gives significant strategic bonuses and attracts allies, which helps toward other victory goals, but winning many battles and accumulating achievements is not itself a means to total victory. Opponents must be conquered, neutralized, or otherwise defeated through the remaining five victory paths. Supersedes all prior content treating it as a sixth path. | 2026-04-07 |
| Victory paths count | SETTLED | Five victory paths: Military Conquest, Economic Dominance (Currency), Faith Divine Right, Territorial Governance, Alliance Victory. Dynastic Prestige eliminated as standalone path. Prestige is now a modifier/bonus system. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Dynastic Prestige as modifier system | SETTLED | Prestige accumulates through bloodline depth, political marriages, famous battles, faith alignment, legendary members, and major diplomatic achievements. High prestige provides strategic bonuses: population loyalty advantages, diplomatic reception improvements, reduced diplomatic friction, attraction of allies and lesser houses. Prestige does not win the game on its own — it accelerates pursuit of the remaining five paths. | 2026-04-07 |
| Phase Entry mode variant | SETTLED | Players can opt to enter the game at any of the five match phases rather than only at the beginning. Entering at Phase 3 means starting at Phase 3 conditions with an accelerated build-up rate for that phase. Same five-phase structure throughout — different entry point, not a different game. This allows shorter, more immediately active play without changing the underlying match architecture. | 2026-04-07 |
| CB004 house profiles — 8 non-Ironmark houses | VOIDED | All CB004 strategic design content for the eight non-Ironmark founding houses is voided and removed from active canon. This includes strategic archetypes, squad traits, unique unit specs, victory path affinities, conviction tendencies, and house narrative depth. Only settled elements remain canonical for these eight houses: hair color, color theme (as derived from hair color), and the lesser houses palette principle. Strategic design for these eight houses is pending future work. Ironmark remains fully locked as the sole exception. | 2026-04-07 |
| Currency Dominance — custom currency model | PROPOSED | Currency Dominance victory is achieved when a dynasty's custom currency displaces gold as the world trade standard. Mechanism: the dynasty introduces its own currency and invests in making gold devalue through inflation while the custom currency gains adoption. When the Trueborn City and other dynasties adopt the custom currency for trade and economy, the dominant dynasty effectively gains unlimited resources — making them effectively unstoppable if the adoption goes unchallenged. Counter: Resolution Battle mechanic (existing) and alliance formation. Not fully locked — full mechanics to be refined later. | 2026-04-07 |
| Alliance trigger conditions | SETTLED | Alliances between players are available but conditional. Two restrictions: (1) Alliances cannot be formed between dynasties at opposite faith extremes. (2) If one dynasty is clearly steamrolling the match, the other players must have the option to counter-ally against that dynasty. This ensures alliances are available as a strategic tool while preventing faith-incompatible coalitions and guaranteeing a counter-coalition mechanic against dominant powers. | 2026-04-07 |
| Wild faith animal mounts expansion | PROPOSED | At a sufficient Wild faith intensity threshold, players can use other animals beyond bears as cavalry mounts. These additional animals provide higher bonuses than horse cavalry. The specific animals, thresholds, and mechanical details are to be explored in depth in a future design session. Bears remain the established anchor. | 2026-04-07 |
| Territorial Governance victory threshold | PROPOSED | Approximately 90% population loyalty threshold — essentially the opposition at that point is nothing more than minor tribes that the winning dynasty is choosing to allow to exist. Exact threshold not locked — fine-tune later through data layer. | 2026-04-07 |
| Victory threshold specification | OPEN | Specific numerical thresholds for all five victory paths are not locked. Faith Divine Right leans toward a higher global faith share requirement (significant conversion investment required). Currency Dominance threshold depends on world adoption model. Territorial Governance is approximately 90% loyalty. Military Conquest remains at 75% territory / 60% stability floor. All thresholds are to be tuned in the data layer. | 2026-04-07 |
| House starting relationships | SETTLED | Seven canonical starting relationships at match start: Goldgrave vs Whitehall (economic rivalry); Ironmark vs Stonehelm (military respect/wariness); Highborne vs Trueborn (prestige competition); Hartvale + Stonehelm (natural partnership: food for defense); Oldcrest vs Westland (cultural friction: conservatism vs frontier impulse); Trueborn vs Blood Dominion dynasties (ideological tension); Whitehall with all houses (universal diplomatic access by design). These are tendencies, not locks. Player action can alter them. | 2026-04-07 |

---

## Thirteenth Session Canon — Declared Time Model and Commander System — 2026-04-07

Source: BLOODLINES_ADDENDUM_A_2026-04-07.md — Addendum A integration.

| Element | Status | Decision | Date |
|---------|--------|----------|------|
| Declared time model | SETTLED | The dual-clock architecture is resolved through declared time. Battles are fought in real-time (20-40 real minutes). When a battle concludes, the game declares the elapsed in-world time (3-6 months for skirmishes, 1-2 years for major pitched battles, 3-5 years for sustained siege campaigns). The player never has to reconcile the two clocks simultaneously — they experience the battle clock during battles and the dynasty clock during the strategic layer between battles. The declaration at battle's end is the seam that joins them. All prior universal year-to-hour ratio approaches and time-pause approaches are rejected and superseded. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Declaration table | PROPOSED | The specific declaration table — mapping engagement type, stage, and outcome to elapsed in-world time — is a primary lever for controlling dynasty pacing. Design target: 2-3 generations achievable in a full 6-10 hour session. Major engagements in Stages Three and Four must declare roughly 2-4 years each to achieve this. Exact values to be specified in a future session. | 2026-04-07 |
| Declaration as narrative beat | SETTLED | The time declaration is presented as a brief narrative statement in the voice of the match world, not as a game-system notification. Not "elapsed time: 2 years" — rather, a statement that reflects what the battle represented in the world's history. Format serves both the time declaration purpose and the story-generator purpose. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Strategic layer architecture | SETTLED | Following each battle declaration, the player enters the strategic layer — the regional and sovereign view of the game. Not a menu or pause screen: it is the game. Two components: (1) Events queue: all dynasty, diplomatic, operational, faith, and conviction events that accumulated during elapsed in-world time. (2) Commitment phase: the player defines all active front orders, Commander directives, operations, diplomatic communications, and bloodline member assignments before dropping into the next battle. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Battle-strategic cycle rhythm | SETTLED | The rhythm of a Bloodlines session is: battle — declaration — events queue — commitment phase — battle. The battles are what the player directly experiences as the RTS game. The strategic layer is where the dynasty simulation lives. The declared time is the seam. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| No dynasty interrupts during battle | SETTLED | Dynasty events and operational events do not interrupt live battles. The player's full attention is on the tactical layer. All events queue and surface at the declaration screen post-battle. Exception: events of immediate existential consequence to the active battle only — Commander killed, supply line cut, bloodline member wounded in active engagement. These surface as tactical intelligence, not management prompts. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Rogue and mystic time model | SETTLED | Covert and faith operations run across multiple battle-strategic cycles in background, not in real session time. A multi-year in-world covert campaign resolves over 1-2 real hours of session time. Mystic calendar windows (Blood Moon, Solstice, etc.) are visible in the strategic layer as in-world time offsets — the player plans battle cycles around them. Missing the window means waiting for the next occurrence. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Shared multiplayer calendar | SETTLED | In multiplayer, a single shared in-world calendar advances based on aggregate match activity, not any individual player's battle frequency. All players receive declarations drawn from the shared calendar, keeping all dynasties in roughly the same generational neighborhood. Match does not pause for any individual player's strategic layer window. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Commander system — foundational principle | SETTLED | Every army is always commandable by the player. There is no hard cap on armies. The limitation is human attention, not a mechanical wall. The Commander system provides genuine delegates — Commanders with real tactical profiles, conviction postures, and faith alignments — that execute directives when the player is in direct control of another battle. Overextension produces degraded outcomes on unattended fronts, not a hard stop. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Commander types | SETTLED | Two types: (1) Bloodline Member Commander — brings full personal history, conviction, faith, and Tactical Memory to the army; amplifies performance above raw unit values; has command capacity threshold; develops over match. (2) AI Field Commander — competent, reliable, generic; executes directives at baseline unit values; does not develop; not subject to assassination or capture operations. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Command capacity | SETTLED | A bloodline Commander's command capacity is the scale of force they can effectively lead at full effectiveness. Armies beyond their capacity are led at degraded effectiveness on the excess units. Capacity grows with development. No hard cap — the player can assign a novice Commander to the largest army. They will simply do it badly. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Directive system | SETTLED | All non-player-controlled armies operate under standing Directives. Directives are set during the commitment phase and persist across battle cycles. Twelve directive categories defined: Territorial (Hold This Line, Advance, Secure Region, Push Front, Fall Back, Contain and Watch), Combat (Eliminate, Suppress, Intercept, Protect Asset), Operational (Secure Resource, Establish Forward Position, Clear Route, Support Operation), Diplomatic (Demonstrate Force, Monitor and Report, Enforce Boundary), Siege (Encircle and Starve, Assault When Ready, Siege and Negotiate). LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Attitude modifiers | SETTLED | Seven attitude modifiers applied on top of any directive: Aggressive, Measured, Cautious, Economical, Relentless, Disciplined, Opportunistic. Modifiers stack with the Commander's personal profile. A bloodline Commander applies modifiers with personal nuance. An AI Field Commander applies them literally. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Direct control toggle | SETTLED | The player can switch any army from Commander direction to direct player control at any time. Toggle is immediate. If switching mid-engagement, the player enters the battle in its current state. The Commander's passive bonuses (morale aura, conviction alignment, faith presence) continue during direct player control. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |
| Commander development | SETTLED | Bloodline Commanders accumulate qualitative campaign experience (type and complexity of engagements led), faith intensity deepening through sustained military service, and conviction record shaped by their tactical history. Legendary Member status is achievable through Commander development. LOCKED. | 2026-04-07 |

======================================================================
# PART B2 — CANON LOCK
======================================================================

# Bloodlines — Canon Lock Reference
**Created:** 2026-03-31 (Phase 5 ecosystem audit)
**Purpose:** Quick-reference for which major design decisions are SETTLED (locked) vs OPEN (undecided). This complements CANONICAL_RULES.md, which has the full granular list. This file focuses on the highest-level decisions that shape the entire game.

---

## How to Use This File

- **LOCKED** means the decision is final. Do not contradict it in any session without explicit revisitation and authorization from Lance.
- **OPEN** means the decision is unresolved. Multiple ideas may coexist. Future sessions may freely propose alternatives.
- **PROPOSED → REVIEW** means the concept has been fully designed in system files but not formally elevated to SETTLED in CANONICAL_RULES.md. These should be reviewed for settlement.

---

## Core Identity

| Decision | Status | Summary |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Game title | LOCKED | Bloodlines |
| Genre | LOCKED | Massively large-scale medieval RTS, Warcraft 3-aligned (lower unit count, micro emphasis) |
| Scope directive | LOCKED | No scope ceiling. Depth is the goal. Every session makes the design richer, not smaller. |
| Tone | LOCKED | Serious, adult, R-rated design sensibility. Sanitized rituals explicitly rejected. |
| Art style | LOCKED | Realistic proportions, natural anatomy, non-cartoon |
| Three pillars | LOCKED | Dynasty (lineage/political), Faith (worldview/cultural), Conviction (moral character) |

Tendencies, not locks — player can change them. Set 2026-04-07. |

| Population system | LOCKED | Unified pool, realm-wide, growth by food+water. 90-second cycle. Loyalty mechanic. |
| Resource system | LOCKED | 6 resources: gold, food, water, wood, stone, iron. Iron added 2026-03-18. |

| Dynastic system | LOCKED | Bloodline active cap 20. Succession by player choice. Deep family management. Mysticism path, sorcerer role. |
| Born of Sacrifice | LOCKED | Population-constrained army lifecycle. Veterans recycled. Elite status through institutional depth. Not a one-time ritual. |

---

## Dual-Clock Architecture (Addendum A — 2026-04-07)

| Decision | Status | Summary |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Declared time model | LOCKED | Battles fought in real-time (20-40 real min). On battle conclusion, game declares elapsed in-world time (3-6 months for skirmishes, 1-2 years for major battles, 3-5 years for sieges). No universal real-to-in-world ratio. Two different clocks: battle clock during battles, dynasty clock during strategic layer. Declaration is the seam. |
| Battle-strategic cycle | LOCKED | Battle → Declaration → Events Queue → Commitment Phase → Battle. This is the rhythm of a Bloodlines session. |
| No dynasty interrupts during battle | LOCKED | All dynasty/operational events queue during live battle. Surface at declaration screen post-battle. Exception: existential in-battle events only (Commander killed, supply line cut, bloodline member wounded). |
| Strategic layer | LOCKED | Post-declaration events queue + commitment phase. Not a menu — it is the game. |
| Shared multiplayer calendar | LOCKED | Single in-world timeline for all players. Calendar advances on aggregate match activity, not individual player's battle frequency. |
| Commander system | LOCKED | No hard cap on armies. Every army always commandable by player. Limitation is human attention. Two commander types: Bloodline Member Commander (full personal history, development, faith, conviction — amplifies above raw unit values) and AI Field Commander (competent, generic, does not develop). |
| Directive system | LOCKED | 14 directives across 5 categories: Territorial, Combat, Operational, Diplomatic, Siege. Persists across battle cycles. Adjusted during commitment phase. |
| Attitude modifiers | LOCKED | 7 modifiers: Aggressive, Measured, Cautious, Economical, Relentless, Disciplined, Opportunistic. Stack with Commander's personal profile. |
| Command capacity | LOCKED | Quality threshold — Commander leads their capacity at full effectiveness, excess at degraded effectiveness. Grows with development. No hard cap prevents the player from overassigning. |
| Direct control toggle | LOCKED | Player can switch any army to direct control at any time, including mid-engagement. Commander passive bonuses (morale aura, faith presence) continue during direct player control. |
| Declaration table | PROPOSED | Specific in-world time per engagement type, stage, and outcome — primary pacing lever. Values to be specified in a future session. |

## Recommendations for Next Canon Session

1. **DESIGN: 8 non-Ironmark house profiles** — hair color and color theme are the only settled elements; full house strategic design pending
2. **DESIGN: Bloodline population/family scope** — high-priority open gap for managing hundreds of members across generations
3. **DESIGN: Personality Trait System** — what traits do Bloodline Members have; how do they express in events
4. **DESIGN: Wild faith animal mounts** — specific animals, thresholds, bonuses beyond bears
5. **SPECIFY: Declaration table** — specific in-world time values per engagement type and stage; the primary pacing lever for dynasty generational timing
6. **SETTLE: Currency Dominance mechanics** — custom currency model defined, full mechanics need design
7. **SETTLE: Spiritual Manifestations** — fully designed in FAITH_SYSTEM, should move from PROPOSED to SETTLED
8. **SPECIFY: Victory thresholds** — lock specific numbers or explicitly categorize as data-layer tuning

# PART C — CORE SYSTEM DEEP-DIVES
======================================================================


--- CONVICTION_SYSTEM ---

# Bloodlines — Conviction System

## Overview
The Conviction system models belief, morale, and ideological commitment as a tangible gameplay mechanic. Conviction is not just a passive stat. It is a resource that can be spent, invested, and lost. It reflects how deeply a faction's people believe in their cause, their faith, and their leaders.

## Design Intent
Conviction exists to create meaningful tension between what a player wants to do and what their people are willing to support. High conviction enables extraordinary actions (including Born of Sacrifice). Low conviction leads to desertion, unrest, and collapse from within.

## Open Design Questions
- How is conviction generated? (Faith actions, victories, leadership traits, sacrifices?)
- How is conviction spent? (Elite unit creation, morale in battle, political actions?)
- How does conviction decay? (Defeats, atrocities, broken promises, leader death?)
- Is conviction per-faction, per-territory, per-army, or per-population group?
- What is the relationship between conviction and faith alignment?

---

*Detailed mechanics will be appended here as design sessions explore this system.*

### Design Content (Added 2026-03-15)

Conviction is a behavioral morality spectrum shaped permanently by actions. It is not a cosmetic alignment label chosen in a menu. It is produced by what the player actually does during the match.

**Canonical Definition:** Bloodline = family. Conviction = philosophy. Faith = religion.

Conviction is distinct from Faith. Faith is chosen. Conviction is lived. Faith determines doctrine and worldview. Conviction determines what kind of civilization the player has actually become through action.

**Actions that influence Conviction:**
- Warfare practices
- Treatment of prisoners
- Enslavement
- Protection of civilians
- Pillage and destruction
- Sacrifice
- Governance decisions

Conviction is permanent and reflects the historical character of the civilization. Conviction represents the moral character of the dynasty's rule. Faith Alignment represents religious practice. These two systems influence each other but remain separate.

**Conviction permanently shapes:**
- Player abilities
- Faith alignment interactions
- Kingdom development
- Late game powers

**Examples of conviction directions:**
- Conquest
- Prosperity through trade
- Faith authority
- Territorial governance
- Dynastic prestige

---

### Player Manual Expansion (Added 2026-03-15)

**Dual Definition Preserved:** Conviction has two definitions that both matter:
1. Conviction is how your dynasty acts — the behavioral morality spectrum shaped permanently by actions and decisions. It does not drift automatically. It includes an endpoint described as cruel and morally bankrupt beyond simply cruel. It affects reputation, loyalty, sabotage risk, and willingness of populations to contribute.
2. Conviction is also the governing philosophy and world-facing strategic path the dynasty pursues — the "how we intend to rule and win" layer. It evolves over time and can shift as the dynasty adapts.

These are not conflicting definitions. They are two layers of the same reality: what you do, and what you become. The dynasty's behavior shapes its moral character and its strategic philosophy.

**Conviction in Practice:** Bloodlines watches for patterns, not isolated incidents. Repeated behavior matters. The realm remembers what kind of power you practice.

**Legitimacy Interaction:** A harsh act committed by a crumbling illegitimate dynasty may provoke revolt. The same act committed by a triumphant prestigious dynasty with strong religious and cultural authority may be interpreted as necessary or justified. Perception changes consequences. Bloodlines is not naive moral math.

---

### Faith and Conviction Independence (Added 2026-03-18)

## Faith and Conviction Independence (LOCKED)

Faith and Conviction are explicitly defined as independent systems. This separation is a CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLE and must never be violated.

**The rule:**

Faith is chosen. Conviction is lived.

Faith defines what a dynasty believes and practices spiritually. Conviction defines what a dynasty has actually become through the sum of its actions.

**Critical Rule — they must never be forced into alignment:**

* A player may follow a dark faith path (Blood Dominion dark expression, brutal sacrifice, blood rites) and still maintain positive conviction through just governance, protection of populations, and honorable warfare.

* A player may follow a light faith path (Old Light mercy expression, ceremonial worship, healing) and still accumulate negative conviction through cruelty to prisoners, pillage, enslavement, and betrayal.

The faith-conviction independence rule exists to prevent the game from becoming a simple moral axis. A player's spiritual practice does not determine their moral character. Actions do.

Faith influences conviction through mechanics (certain faith actions shift conviction), but the player can counteract those influences through other actions. The two systems interact but are never locked together.

**Design implications:**

The most interesting rulers are those whose faith and conviction are in tension. A dynasty that follows the Blood Dominion with genuine mercy creates a different civilization than one that follows it with cruelty. A dynasty that follows the Old Light as a tool of control becomes something different from one that follows it as genuine stewardship. This tension is a feature, not a conflict to be resolved.

---

### Conviction Axis Model and Three Pillars (Added 2026-03-18)

## Conviction and Faith — Axis Model

Civilizations in Bloodlines are defined by two independent axes:

**Faith Doctrine Axis:**
* Light Doctrine
* Dark Doctrine

**Conviction Axis:**
* High Conviction
* Neutral Conviction
* Low Conviction

These axes are independent. No position on the Faith Doctrine Axis implies any position on the Conviction Axis.

## Conviction — Design Intent

Conviction measures:
* integrity
* consistency
* treatment of others
* exercise of power

It reflects how a ruler behaves, not what they believe.

## Example Outcomes

**Dark Faith + High Conviction:**
* Structured, disciplined, internally respected societies
* Harsh but principled
* A civilization that practices blood rites with genuine conviction, internal order, and principled governance

**Light Faith + Low Conviction:**
* Corrupt, hypocritical, unstable societies
* Externally righteous but internally broken
* A civilization that performs ceremonial worship while abusing its populations

## Final Design Structure — Three Pillars

Every civilization in Bloodlines is defined by three pillars:

1. **Dynasty** — defines lineage and political identity
2. **Faith** — defines worldview and cultural systems
3. **Conviction** — defines moral character and leadership integrity

Dynasty defines who you are. Faith defines what you believe. Conviction defines how you rule.

These three pillars are independent of each other. Any combination is valid and produces a distinct civilization.

---

### Conviction Terminology — Moral/Cruel Axis (Added 2026-03-18, Fourth Ingestion)

**LOCKED: The Conviction Axis terminology is updated from High/Neutral/Low to Moral/Neutral/Cruel.**

**Updated Conviction Axis:**
* Moral Conviction
* Neutral Conviction
* Cruel Conviction

The terms "High" and "Low" are deprecated and replaced. "Moral" and "Cruel" are the canonical terms going forward.

The prior axis model entry (High/Neutral/Low) is preserved above as historical record. This entry supersedes it.

**Why this matters:** "High" and "Low" implied an abstract meter. "Moral" and "Cruel" name the actual character of the ruler and civilization. These are not measurements — they are identities. The population, the Trueborn city, and other dynasties do not see a number. They see a ruler who is either principled or merciless.

---

### Conviction as a Central Game Feature (Added 2026-03-18, Fourth Ingestion)

Conviction is not a background system. It is a prominent, featured aspect of gameplay with significant mechanical weight.

**Impact on Population:**
- Moral conviction generates population loyalty, volunteer recruitment, and willingness to contribute beyond what is conscripted
- Cruel conviction generates fear-driven compliance but increases desertion risk, suppresses birth rates, and eliminates volunteer contributions
- Population remembers patterns of rule — a sudden cruel act after years of moral rule carries different weight than prolonged cruelty
- The population does not react to isolated acts in isolation; it reacts to the accumulated character of the dynasty

**Impact on the Trueborn City:**
- The Trueborn city maintains a conviction record for all players across the match
- A dynasty with extreme cruel conviction risks triggering the Trueborn coalition response
- A dynasty with sustained moral conviction earns favorable treatment: trade bonuses, diplomatic access, potential coalition membership
- The Trueborn are not naive — a moral conviction record that is recent after a history of cruelty receives less weight

**Impact on Other Dynasty Leaders:**
- Other leaders perceive and react to conviction record throughout the match
- Moral rulers attract alliances, diplomatic respect, and the voluntary defection of enemy populations
- Cruel rulers face diplomatic isolation, heightened espionage risk, and accelerated coalition formation against them
- Conviction reputation is one of the components of a dynasty's standing in the world — it cannot be easily falsified

---

### Conviction Milestones (Added 2026-03-18, Fourth Ingestion)

Conviction milestones are unlocked at specific thresholds on both ends of the axis. Both directions carry genuine power. Neither is a trap.

**Moral Conviction Milestones:**
- Population morale bonus: troops drawn from a moral population fight with higher base morale
- Volunteer recruitment: population offers troops without conscription cost or loyalty penalty
- Trade network trust bonus: trade partners extend favorable terms to dynasties with moral conviction records
- Diplomatic favor with the Trueborn city: access to coalition membership, mediation services, and Trueborn trade routes
- Light doctrine faith amplification: moral conviction strengthens the effects of light doctrine faith mechanics
- Extreme moral threshold: potential divine favor manifestation (light-aligned entity interventions, healing amplification, morale events)
- Other leaders more likely to sue for peace rather than fight to the death against a moral dynasty

**Cruel Conviction Milestones:**
- Fear compliance: population suppresses internal dissent, reducing revolt risk in exchange for lower voluntary contribution
- Enemy morale penalty on contact: the dynasty's reputation for cruelty degrades opposing troop morale before battle begins
- Prisoner execution option: executing prisoners unlocks a resource return not available through ransom or release
- Dark doctrine amplification: cruel conviction strengthens the effects of dark doctrine faith mechanics
- Unbeliever sacrifice threshold: at extreme cruel conviction combined with high dark doctrine faith intensity, the sacrifice of unbelieving population becomes available (see Faith System — Dark Doctrine)
- Trueborn coalition preparation begins: the Trueborn city begins forming a response coalition as extreme cruel conviction is sustained
- Other leaders more likely to pre-emptively attack a cruel dynasty out of calculated fear rather than wait to be targeted

---

### What Conviction Offers the Ruler and People (Added 2026-03-18, Fourth Ingestion)

**For the Ruler:**
- Moral conviction builds legitimacy — the dynasty's right to rule is accepted rather than enforced
- Moral conviction opens diplomatic paths unavailable to cruel rulers: voluntary territorial integration, alliance formation, Trueborn support
- Cruel conviction opens power paths unavailable to moral rulers: unbeliever sacrifice, fear-based army composition, extreme dark doctrine mechanics
- Conviction record shapes which victory paths are viable — a cruel dynasty cannot win through governance attraction; a moral dynasty cannot unlock certain dark faith escalations
- Extreme conviction in either direction unlocks late-game powers that neutral dynasties cannot access

**For the People:**
- Population under moral conviction grows faster, contributes more willingly, and reaches higher faith intensity
- Population under moral conviction generates volunteer military units without recruiting cost
- Population under cruel conviction shrinks faster, requires enforcement infrastructure to maintain, and generates no volunteers
- Population under cruel conviction may comply — but the compliance is transactional, not loyal
- The composition and quality of a dynasty's population is directly shaped by the conviction of its rulers over time

---

### Light/Dark Faith Specific Conviction Interactions (Added 2026-03-18, Fourth Ingestion)

The four possible combinations of faith doctrine axis and conviction axis each produce a distinct civilization type with distinct mechanics:

**Light Doctrine + Moral Conviction:**
- Maximum faith intensity amplification
- Maximum population goodwill and loyalty
- Access to healing and restoration mechanics
- Volunteer recruitment bonus stacked with faith intensity bonus
- Potential divine manifestation: angelic or light-entity interventions, population morale events
- Strongest diplomatic position in the game

**Light Doctrine + Cruel Conviction:**
- Internal religious hypocrisy — faith intensity degrades over time as the population perceives the faith as a tool of control
- Reduced faith intensity caps relative to moral conviction
- Diplomatic damage among other dynasties following the same light faith
- Risk of religious fracture: a segment of the population may organize around authentic faith practice and resist the regime
- The gap between professed values and practiced cruelty creates instability that worsens over time

**Dark Doctrine + Moral Conviction:**
- Structured, principled practitioners of a harsh faith — rare and formidable
- Access to dark doctrine mechanics without the worst cruel conviction penalties
- Population is not oppressed but is part of a demanding, ordered system with genuine internal justice
- Unique diplomatic position: feared but respected
- Other dynasties cannot easily predict or dismiss this civilization — it defies the expected categories
- Faith intensity remains stable because the conviction and the doctrine are internally consistent

**Dark Doctrine + Cruel Conviction:**
- Maximum dark power amplification — both systems reinforce each other
- Access to unbeliever sacrifice at extreme thresholds (see Faith System)
- Fear-based control is maximized throughout the population
- Trueborn coalition formation risk is at its highest
- Enemy morale damage on contact is maximized
- Potential dark manifestation: demonic or corruption-aligned entity interventions
- The most recognizable "dark empire" civilization type in the game — powerful, but encircled

---

### Conviction Action Weight Framework (Added 2026-03-18, Fifth Session)

Conviction is tracked through a point system. Player actions generate conviction points (CP). Accumulated CP determines position on the Moral / Neutral / Cruel axis. The system tracks patterns, not isolated incidents. Repeated behavior compounds.

**Design Rule:** No single action locks a dynasty into any position. Every shift can be counteracted through subsequent behavior — but the accumulated weight of a pattern is harder to reverse than it was to build.

#### Actions That Shift Toward Moral

| Action | CP Shift | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| Release prisoners without ransom | +2 | Minor; generous but economically negligible |
| Honor a peace agreement when breaking it would be advantageous | +5 | Significant; sacrifice of opportunity |
| Protect civilian zones during conflict (no pillage order) | +3 per territory | Moderate; consistent behavior required |
| Veteran retirement with land grant | +2 per cycle | Minor; cumulative across many Born of Sacrifice recycling cycles |
| Rebuild a destroyed territory (yours or conquered) | +4 | Visible investment in human welfare |
| Provide food aid to a starving allied or neutral population | +6 | High; genuine sacrifice of resources |
| Allow conquered population to maintain their faith | +3 | Tolerance exercised under power |
| Heal wounded prisoners before ransom | +1 | Small; basic decency |
| Execute a traitor through public trial (with evidence) | +2 | Justice performed visibly and legitimately |

#### Actions That Shift Toward Cruel

| Action | CP Shift | Notes |
|--------|----------|-------|
| Execute prisoners of war | -4 | Significant; eliminates ransom and diplomatic value |
| Pillage a territory | -3 per territory | Moderate; cumulative if done repeatedly |
| Discard veterans without recognition or reward | -1 per cycle | Minor individually; compounds through Born of Sacrifice cycles |
| Enslave conquered population | -6 | Major; clear act of dehumanization |
| Burn farmland during war | -5 | Devastating to civilian population; visible moral crime |
| Force faith conversion at sword point | -5 | Violates the faith-conviction separation mechanic; also destabilizes target faith intensity |
| Execute a bloodline member of a defeated dynasty | -8 | Very significant; eliminates diplomatic options permanently |
| Sacrifice unbeliever population (requires extreme threshold unlock) | -15 | Catastrophic single-act shift; the most powerful cruel action in the game |
| Conduct a raid specifically targeting water infrastructure | -7 | Attacks survival; universally condemned |
| Imprison an innocent (false accusation of treason) | -4 | Injustice against the dynasty's own people |

#### Neutral Zone Actions (No CP Shift)

| Action | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| Ransom prisoners at market rate | Standard exchange; no moral weight |
| Conscript troops through family obligation | Expected; not cruel |
| Enforce taxation at standard rates | Governance; neutral |
| Execute a guilty traitor without trial | Edge case — shifts slightly cruel if it becomes a pattern |

#### Pattern Amplification Rules

Actions that cross a threshold into pattern behavior have their CP values amplified:

- **First occurrence:** Full CP value as listed
- **Second occurrence (same action within the same stage):** 1.5x CP value
- **Third and subsequent occurrences:** 2x CP value — the realm interprets this as policy, not incident

**The reverse also applies:** A moral action performed only once carries its listed value. A moral action performed consistently across many game cycles becomes the dynasty's character — and generates a reputation bonus that functions independently of the CP total.

#### Conviction Threshold Bands

Specific numeric thresholds are to be tuned in the data layer, but the structural design is as follows:

| Band | Label | Access |
|------|-------|--------|
| Very high positive | Apex Moral | All moral milestones unlocked; divine manifestation eligible; maximum Trueborn favor |
| Moderate positive | Moral | Moral milestones active; trade bonuses; volunteer recruitment |
| Near zero (either side) | Neutral | No bonus, no penalty; maximum diplomatic flexibility |
| Moderate negative | Cruel | Cruel milestones active; fear compliance; prisoner execution bonus |
| Very high negative | Apex Cruel | All cruel milestones unlocked; unbeliever sacrifice eligible; Trueborn coalition active |

--- FAITH_SYSTEM ---

# Bloodlines — Faith System

## Overview
The Faith system defines the four ancient faiths that shape the spiritual and mechanical landscape of the game world. Each faith is not merely aesthetic or narrative. It provides distinct gameplay mechanics, unique abilities, and strategic options that fundamentally alter how a faction operates.

## Design Intent
Faith is a worldview that constrains and empowers. Choosing a faith (or inheriting one through dynasty) opens certain paths and closes others. Faith interacts with conviction, sacrifice, population loyalty, and territorial control. It is one of the primary axes of faction asymmetry.

## The Four Ancient Faiths
*(Names, tenets, and mechanics to be defined in design sessions)*

See `07_FAITHS/FOUR_ANCIENT_FAITHS.md` for detailed faith definitions.

## Open Design Questions
- What are the four faiths and what do they represent?
- Can a faction change faith? At what cost?
- How does faith interact with the conviction system?
- Do faiths have exclusive units, buildings, or abilities?
- Can multiple faiths coexist within a single faction's territory?
- How does faith affect diplomacy between factions?

---

*Detailed mechanics will be appended here as design sessions explore this system.*

### Design Content (Added 2026-03-15)

The faith system is one of the central pillars of Bloodlines and is not a decorative or secondary layer. It is a major strategic axis.

Faith is selected after the early exploration phase (at the end of Level 1 / Stage 1) when dynasties encounter the wider world, tribes, ruins, and sacred sites. The dynasty does not create a faith. It aligns with an existing covenant. The Four Covenants already exist in the world.

**Three Interacting Components:**
1. **Covenant** — the religion the dynasty follows
2. **Faith Intensity** — the degree to which the population actively practices the faith
3. **Faith Alignment** — a sliding scale representing how the covenant is practiced along a light vs dark spectrum

**The Four Covenants:**

**The Old Light (Covenant of the First Flame)** — Protection, mercy, divine guardianship, defense of innocents, enlightenment, unity, societal cohesion, disciplined governance, defensive stability.

**The Blood Dominion (The Red Covenant)** — Conquest, sacrifice, domination, belief that strength demonstrates divine favor. Darker in tone, carries moral cost and conviction consequences, provides strong military benefits.

**The Order (Covenant of the Sacred Mandate)** — Law, doctrine, institutional authority, structured governance under divine mandate, rigid hierarchy, administrative control.

**The Wild (Covenant of the Thorned Elder Roots)** — Nature mysticism, ancient spirits, balance of the natural world, adaptive survival, environmental strength, ancient rooted power.

**Faith Intensity:**
- Must be maintained (not a passive permanent buff)
- Costs resources
- Scales with population participation
- Higher intensity unlocks stronger abilities
- Grows through participation, rituals, structures, and engagement
- As faith grows stronger it can radicalize

**Faith Alignment (Light vs Dark):**
- Same faith can be interpreted differently by different kingdoms
- Example: Blood Dominion practiced with symbolic offerings vs brutal sacrificial rites
- Large alignment differences within same covenant can cause internal religious conflict and schisms

**Faith Actions (consume resources, may consume captured units or population):**
- Rituals
- Offerings
- Temple construction
- Priesthood expansion
- Religious festivals
- Sacrificial rites

**Spiritual Manifestations (at high intensity):**
- Angels, demons, cursed armies, healing, resurrection, plague removal, nature forces
- World-level pressure from extreme dark behavior

**Faith Buildings:**
- Shrine — small religious structure increasing faith intensity
- Temple — major religious structure enabling powerful rituals
- Grand Sanctuary — late-game structure tied to faith victory

---

### Player Manual Expansion (Added 2026-03-15)

**Terminology:** Faith replaces any earlier use of the word "arcane." The spiritual system is called Faith, anchored to four covenants.

**House Neutrality at Start:** Houses have no inherent leaning. Houses do not start aligned. This prevents pre-locking before the map context is known.

**Faith Intensity Maintenance:** There is no automatic drift toward neutrality beyond consequences of neglect. Faith is maintained or neglected. Faith intensity changes by actions and maintenance, clearly understood by the player based on decisions and triggers.

**Progressive Scaling:** Faith costs can scale progressively based on how much of the population practices the faith. Higher practicing population increases scale, cost, and consequences of major faith actions.

**Level 4 Faith Divergence:** At Level 4, a player can commit to hardline fanatical expression of their Covenant, triggering extreme behaviors and unlocking grand structures and apex mechanics. Level 5 contains endgame grand structures and capabilities specific and exceptional based on prior choices.

**Spiritual Manifestations Expanded:**
- Spirits of fallen bloodline generals returning during war as angels or demons
- Evil faith at high levels can spawn a demon that corrupts an army and turns it bloodlusted, sometimes going on campaigns on its own
- These manifestations are rare and powerful, tied to faith intensity and world state

**Extreme Dark Faith World Pressure:** Extreme and continual dark actions can create a world pressure situation where population decline becomes severe and potentially irreversible unless other houses act. The narrative tone is that the dark faith and cruel actions have escalated beyond tolerable limits and must be stopped. This produces systemic pressure for conflict, coalitions, and forced wars.

**Covenant Gameplay Depth (from Player Manual Parts 25, 47-50):**

*The Old Light:* Not passive. Late-game access to holy armies, blessed warriors, healing, resurrection, plague removal, and sacred military seriousness. Strongest when paired with disciplined governance and real military competence. Mercy without strength is fragile; strength without integrity betrays the covenant. Not dependent on culture integration requirements. About stewardship and protection.

*The Blood Dominion:* Strongest when played with disciplined brutality, not indiscriminate madness. The most dangerous ruler knows exactly when terror advances power and when terror invites premature retaliation. Consequences include isolation, fear, diplomatic refusal, external hostility, and coalition-triggering. Fanatical states may take no prisoners in conquest.

*The Order:* Power comes from building structures of obedience and meaning that outlast individual engagements. Well suited to dynasties that want to make the world feel administratively and spiritually claimed. Danger: can become rigid or coercive without actual strength. A dead bureaucracy with holy language is still dead.

*The Wild:* Not a narrow "druid" theme. Can become terrifying strategic identity. Especially powerful in certain terrains and for dynasties willing to let conflict become less orderly. Best Wild dynasties understand how to align people, land, and bloodline with something older and more dangerous than institutions.

---

### Faith Duality System (Added 2026-03-18)

## Faith Duality — Dark and Light Expression Paths (LOCKED)

Each of the four ancient covenants contains both a dark expression path and a light expression path. This duality applies to ALL four faiths without exception.

The two paths represent how a covenant is practiced, not what it believes. The same faith can produce radically different civilizations depending on the expression path the player pursues.

**Critical Rule:** Faith path (dark or light) and Conviction are separate and must never be forced into alignment. A player may follow a dark faith expression with positive conviction. A player may follow a light faith expression with negative conviction.

**Blood Dominion — Expression Paths:**

Dark Path:
* Ritual sacrifice of enemies
* Blood ceremonies
* Dedicated altars
* Population consuming blood during ritual

Light Path:
* Animal sacrifice instead of human
* Structured ceremonial worship
* Resource trade-offs (food impact)
* Priest and priestess development

**The Old Light — Expression Paths:**

Dark Path:
* Judgment and punishment as divine mandate
* Forced conversion and purging of heresy
* Military crusades with religious justification
* Harsh governance framed as protection

Light Path:
* Mercy, healing, and protection of innocents
* Holy armies and blessed warriors
* Disciplined governance and societal cohesion
* Resurrection and plague removal

**The Order — Expression Paths:**

Dark Path:
* Coercive obedience through fear
* Inquisitions and enforcement of doctrine
* Administrative control as instrument of terror
* Rigid hierarchy enforced by punishment

Light Path:
* Law as structure for prosperity
* Institutional authority through genuine governance
* Divine mandate expressed through competence
* Ordered civilization as spiritual expression

**The Wild — Expression Paths:**

Dark Path:
* Ancient spirits weaponized against enemies
* Environmental destruction turned to strategic advantage
* Bloodlusted nature forces deployed in war
* Territory rendered hostile and impassable to outsiders

Light Path:
* Balance of natural world maintained
* Adaptive survival and environmental strength
* Ancient rooted power used for protection
* Land and bloodline aligned with older forces

This duality ensures that no covenant is morally pre-locked. Players may pursue any expression of any faith and combine it with any conviction direction.

---

### Doctrine Path System and Expanded Examples (Added 2026-03-18)

## Doctrine Path System

Faith includes a second layer called the Doctrine Path. The terminology "Doctrine Path" refers to the same dark/light expression path system established above. Each Faith contains two cultural implementations: Light Doctrine and Dark Doctrine.

These are not separate faiths. They are divergent expressions of the same belief system.

The Doctrine Path system exists to:
* create diversity within the same faith
* introduce internal ideological conflict
* enable narrative depth
* avoid forcing rigid playstyles
* support interaction with Conviction

## Blood Dominion — Expanded Doctrine Detail

Dark Doctrine:
* Ritual sacrifice of enemies
* Elaborate blood ceremonies
* Construction of blood altars
* Population participation in blood rites
* Possible sacrifice of bloodline members

Ceremonies require specific structures, time to perform, and population involvement. These rituals can produce powerful effects.

Light Doctrine:
* Animal sacrifice instead of human sacrifice
* Ceremonial gatherings tied to celestial events
* Resource cost impacts such as reduced food supply
* Development of priesthood roles within the bloodline

Bloodline members may become priests or priestesses devoted to the faith. Large ritual structures may be constructed.

## Faith Spread and Global Influence

Faith can spread across populations beyond a single civilization.

Methods of spread may include:
* cultural influence
* conquest
* missionary activity
* shared borders

If a single Faith becomes dominant globally, late-game religious conflict can occur.

Possible outcomes:
* holy wars
* doctrinal conflicts
* civilizational division

---

### Full System Design Expansion — Sessions 3-9 (Added 2026-03-19)

NOTE: The following supersedes prior bullet-point summaries above in cases of conflict. Prior entries preserved per archive rules.

---

## Section 1: The Four Covenants — Identity and Philosophy

### The Old Light

At its core, the Old Light believes that civilization is the highest expression of the divine will — not a symbol of it, not a shadow of it, but the thing itself. Before The Great Frost, there were cities, laws, accumulated knowledge, roads that connected communities, institutions that carried memory beyond the lifespan of any single person. The Frost did not merely kill people. It erased what those people had built together. The Old Light looks at that erasure and names it the defining catastrophe of all existence. Their theology is built on that naming.

What the Old Light believes, precisely: the divine is not something above the world or outside of it. The divine was expressed through civilization's existence, and it was withdrawn — or tested, or punished; the covenant debates this — when civilization collapsed. The obligation that falls on every Old Light dynasty is reconstruction. Not merely survival. Not merely comfort. The deliberate, disciplined, costly effort of rebuilding the kind of civilization that does not collapse again, that maintains its institutional memory, that produces law and order and continuity as a sacred duty rather than a political convenience.

This is a faith that attracts dynasties that understand governance as a vocation. Dynasties that believe the work of ordering society is not merely practical but morally serious. The Old Light tends toward cultures with long memory — families that kept records through the Frost, bloodlines that preserved the names and deeds of the dead, communities that built libraries before they built fortifications. It tends toward cultures that distrust improvisation and value procedure, because they understand that what collapsed was not fortified enough against the chaos that destroyed it.

The Old Light produces civilizations marked by: codified law that is publicly known and consistently applied; architectural permanence — structures built to last generations, not seasons; institutions that outlast individual leaders; a serious relationship with the written record; and governance that treats its obligations as genuine rather than performed.
