- Shared Wound: The Covenant Priest may voluntarily absorb a portion of damage dealt to an adjacent allied unit — 40% of what would have been the full hit is distributed to the Priest instead. The Priest bleeds so the covenant holds.
- Covenant Witness: Deaths within 3 hexes of the Covenant Priest contribute to a Covenant Stack. At 5 deaths, the Priest activates Covenant Surge — all allied units within 4 hexes gain +22% combat effectiveness for 3 cycles. The dead are paying forward.

**Covenant Scion (Level 4, Light Doctrine)**
The Covenant Scion has undergone the Covenant Scar rite in addition to full martial training. They have given and cannot take it back.
- Scarred Will: Immune to morale breaks. Cannot route. When reduced below 30% health, the penalty state is replaced by Scarred Will — combat stats increase 18% for 5 cycles. Near-death triggers rather than diminishes.
- Bonding Aura: Units under the Scion's command accumulate Bond Score at double rate. Units that have served under the Scion for 5+ cycles gain the Scion-Bonded trait permanently, which persists through reassignment.
- The Price Paid: Once per battle, the Scion converts all remaining health above 1 point into a restoration surge for all allied units within 5 hexes. The health spent translates to morale and health restoration proportionally distributed across the affected units. The Scion survives at 1 health point. This is a tactical sacrifice mechanic. Its value depends entirely on when it is used.

**Apex unit — The Sacrificed (Light Doctrine expression):**
The Light Doctrine Sacrificed is a figure covered in healed covenant scars — armor repaired so many times it is more repair than original material, moving with the deliberateness of someone who knows exactly how close the edge is because they have been there and came back. This is not a warrior who was spared. This is a warrior the covenant held because the covenant had been sufficiently paid.

Stats profile: Very high health relative to other apex units, but starts each engagement at 60% of maximum health — the cost of its nature is already paid. At or below 60% health: +30% damage. At or below 40%: +50% damage. At or below 20%: near-immunity to morale effects, +70% damage. The Sacrificed gets stronger as it is hurt because the covenant paid its debt precisely at the moment the body tried to fail.

Unique ability — The Final Covenant: Once per match, The Sacrificed invokes every sacrifice the dynasty has made. For 10 cycles, no allied unit within 6 hexes can be reduced below 1 health point — they can be immobilized, stunned, captured, but cannot die. If The Sacrificed itself is killed during this window, it distributes its remaining health pool equally across all allied units in the radius before it falls. The covenant continues after the one who held it.

---

### Dark Doctrine — The Blood Rite

**The doctrine's logic:**

The Blood Rite path has absorbed the Covenant Bond's theology fully. It agrees that the covenant must be paid, that cost is the mechanism through which the bond becomes real, that sacrifice is not cruelty but stewardship. It has drawn a different conclusion about who pays. The weak exist within the covenant as material. The strong exist within it as architects. Asking the weak to opt out of their contribution is like asking the earth to decline to accept the grain that's sown in it — the question is categorically wrong. The covenant is not a negotiation between equals. It is a hierarchy of purpose, and the Blood Rite priest who stands before the altar and performs the Tithe is not committing violence. They are completing the covenant's structure.

This doctrine does not hide from itself. Its rituals are performed publicly. Its priests do not dissemble about what they are doing. What makes the Blood Rite path genuinely disturbing is not that it is secretly ashamed of itself — it is that it is completely at peace with itself, and that peace is indistinguishable from certainty, and certainty at scale looks identical to institutions that have made atrocity orderly and gave it a title and a calendar.

**Rituals:**

**The Tithe of the Weak**
- Building required: Blood-Altar (Tier 1) or higher, Blood Rider unit present as enforcer
- Who participates: Blood Rider oversees; low-loyalty population of the designated settlement provide the material; Blood-Altar clergy conduct the rite
- What is consumed: 30 Labor (the tithers — an abstraction representing a specific number of individuals from the settlement's lowest-loyalty tier), 15 Gold (the apparatus), 12 Rare Materials. The settlement's population count decreases.
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Blood Rider unit gains a Tithe-Mark for 8 cycles — a visible status in their unit portrait indicating their role in the rite. The remaining population's loyalty paradoxically stabilizes post-Tithe: the doctrine has told them the Tithe selected those who were weakest and least faithful. The survivors understand themselves as having been evaluated and retained.
- Mechanical effect: Faith intensity in the settlement +10%. All military units stationed in the settlement gain +18% combat morale for 12 cycles. A Blood Reserve token is generated, which can be spent to enhance one unit permanently with a single additional stat point. Neighboring factions with Old Light or Order faith intensity above Devout generate a Horror event if line of sight to the settlement exists, reducing their trade willingness toward the Blood Rite dynasty by -12%.
- Faith intensity change: +8%

**The Blood Convocation**
- Building required: Covenant Hall (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Senior clergy officiate; captive enemy soldiers, enslaved populations, or conscripted civilians constitute the ritual material. A Bloodline Member must be present for the Convocation to reach its maximum scale.
- What is consumed: 55 Provisions, 35 Rare Materials, 25 Gold, 25 Labor. The scale of the Convocation determines its output — more material sacrificed produces more Blood Reserve tokens.
- Duration: 4 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Bloodline Member present carries the Convocation Mark for 20 cycles — a visible status that increases their authority within the faction (the population has watched them preside over something definitive). Up to 5 units can be permanently enhanced with Blood Reserve tokens from a single Convocation.
- Mechanical effect: Blood Reserve tokens generated equal to scale of participation. Each token enhances one unit permanently. All Blood Dominion units in the territory gain +22% combat morale for 18 cycles. The Convocation is visible to neighboring factions and triggers Horror responses up to 10 hexes away.
- Faith intensity change: +12%
- Interruption: If disrupted, all captive material escapes. A Convocation Disrupted event generates -18% faith intensity and a domestic Conviction penalty — the failed rite reads as the covenant refusing what was offered.

**The Rite of Dominion**
- Building required: Covenant Hall (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Covenant officiant declares the rite; all Bloodline Members must acknowledge it; no external participation required or permitted
- What is consumed: 45 Gold, 35 Rare Materials, 12 Faith Currency. The rite is a formal theological declaration of hierarchy.
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: All Bloodline Members gain +12% to combat statistics for 22 cycles. Blood Rider units gain the Dominion Mark — visible in portraits and carrying a passive +6% damage bonus for 22 cycles.
- Mechanical effect: Enemy diplomatic overtures during the rite's active period are penalized at -22% effectiveness. The theology has rendered the dynasty genuinely indifferent to external moral frameworks. Faith intensity +8%.
- Interruption: None. The Rite of Dominion is internal.

**The Wound's Hunger**
- Building required: The Wound (Tier 3)
- Who participates: The Wound's clergy; captive enemies as material; a Bloodline Member must personally deliver the first sacrifice to initiate the rite at full scale
- What is consumed: 65 Gold, 55 Rare Materials, 45 Provisions, 35 Faith Currency. Upkeep during active period: 8 Gold and 5 Provisions per cycle. The Wound becomes an active site of mass sacrifice for the duration.
- Duration: 3 cycles to establish, active for up to 12 cycles while captives are delivered
- What happens to participants: Each sacrifice delivered to The Wound during the active period generates: +5% health for all Blood Dominion units currently on the field (distributed equally), +3% faith intensity, one Blood Reserve token. If more than 20 captives are sacrificed in a single activation, the rite escalates into Grand Feeding: The Wound enters a 5-cycle state in which all Blood Dominion units deal +28% damage and Bloodbound units temporarily gain their full apex combat package.
- Mechanical effect: The faith is being fed in real time. The scale is limited only by how many captives the dynasty can supply.
- Faith intensity change: +16% base, +5% per 5 captives above 20
- Interruption: If The Wound is damaged during the Hunger, the rite produces half effects. If destroyed during the Hunger, a catastrophic faith backlash triggers: -22% faith intensity and region-wide panic.

**Passive doctrine effects:**

- Latent (0-20%): No doctrine effects.
- Active (20-40%): Blood Reserve tokens accumulate passively from enemy kills in military actions. Each kill generates a fractional token — active combat maintains the covenant's material supply. Blood Rider units are available.
- Devout (40-60%): Captive enemies generate Blood Reserve tokens at double rate when brought to faith buildings. Low-loyalty populations in home territory experience 22% lower rebellion probability. This is mechanically distinct from satisfaction-based loyalty: it is compliance maintained by the population's understanding of what non-compliance costs.
- Fervent (60-80%): All Blood Dominion units gain passive health regeneration of 2% per cycle during combat — the covenant sustains what it has claimed. Blood Convocation rites cost 30% fewer resources. Horror events generated by Blood Rite rituals affect enemy populations up to 14 hexes away.
- Apex (80-100%): Human sacrifice no longer costs Faith Currency — the bureaucracy of sanctified killing has been normalized to the point of self-sustaining overhead. Enemy populations in adjacent territories experience faith conversion pressure toward Blood Dominion at -3% of their native faith per cycle. The conversion mechanism is not attraction. It is proximity to something that keeps eating.
- Sustained Apex (10+ cycles): The Hungry Covenant activates dynasty-wide. Every battle victory generates a Blood Reserve token regardless of casualties. The covenant feeds on victory itself. The distinction between offering and conquest has collapsed.

**Spiritual manifestation — The Hunger (Fervent+):**

The Hunger does not arrive. It emerges. When Blood Dominion reaches Fervent intensity on the dark doctrine path and at least one Blood Convocation has been completed in the past 20 cycles, the sky above The Wound darkens — not like night, but like the air itself has absorbed what has been poured into the ground beneath it. The color is deep red-brown, diffuse, total within the affected radius. There is a sound: low, rhythmic, like a pulse heard from inside the walls. Not a heartbeat. Slower.

Mechanically, The Hunger creates a zone of effect centered on The Wound extending 9 hexes in every direction. Within this zone, enemy units suffer -3% maximum health per cycle — not damage, but a reduction in their health ceiling. This is cumulative. A unit that spends 10 cycles within The Hunger has permanently lost 30% of its maximum health for the duration of the manifestation. Units that die within the zone generate Blood Reserve tokens at double rate. Blood Dominion units within the zone regenerate health 6% faster per cycle.

Duration: 15 cycles. Unlike other manifestations, The Hunger does not end when the faith building is destroyed. If The Wound is destroyed during the Hunger, the manifestation localizes to the rubble — the 9-hex radius stays centered on the building's location regardless of structural state. It can be ended early only by a specific Order faith ritual or by an Old Light Recitation of the Fallen directed specifically at the territory with Cathedral-level intensity.

**Doctrine-specific unit abilities:**

**Blood Rider (Level 3, Dark Doctrine)**
The Blood Rider has been consecrated to the covenant through personal ritual scarring conducted before their first deployment. They are faster than Covenant Priests and their orientation is entirely combat.
- Blood Price: Each enemy kill generates a charge. At 3 charges, the Blood Rider activates a Covenant Surge — next attack at 155% normal damage, generating a Blood Reserve token.
- Marked for the Covenant: Once per battle, the Blood Rider marks an enemy unit as sacrifice target. All Blood Dominion units deal +18% damage to the marked target. If the marked unit is killed by any Blood Dominion unit, the killing unit receives full health restoration.
- Red Charge: In a charge action, damage dealt is proportional to approach distance. Maximum charge distance: +42% damage.

**Bloodbound (Level 4, Dark Doctrine)**
The Bloodbound has completed the full covenant sacrifice rite at The Wound, giving a significant portion of their own blood in exchange for covenant reinforcement. The exchange was answered. They are physically different from what they were before.
- Covenant Flesh: Health and damage are permanently 18% higher than equivalent Level 4 units because the covenant sacrifice was answered with physical return.
- Unquenchable Thirst: Regenerates 5% health per enemy unit killed, unconditionally, including during ongoing combat actions.
- Blood Debt: Once per battle, invokes a fraction of The Hunger effect within 3 hexes for 3 cycles. Enemy units in this radius suffer the health ceiling reduction at 50% rate.

**Apex unit — The Sacrificed (Dark Doctrine expression):**
The Dark Doctrine Sacrificed has not paid the covenant price themselves. They are the covenant's instrument — a warrior who has fed it so thoroughly that it has remade them. The armor is deep red-black, absorbing light. The scarring is raised and flushed, not healed silver. The movement is wrong in a specific way: too fluid, the hesitations of a body that fears injury absent. This is what a person becomes when the covenant has finished with them and decided to keep them.

Stats profile: Higher base damage than the Light Doctrine version — +27% at full health. Health regeneration at 8% per cycle rather than 5%. Does not scale stronger when injured — fights at peak performance when whole. This is the inverse of the light doctrine's vulnerability arc. The dark version does not need to be hurt to reach its potential. It just needs targets.

Unique ability — The Harvest: Once per match, initiates a 5-cycle Harvest state. Every unit killed within 6 hexes of The Sacrificed — allied or enemy — generates +3% health for The Sacrificed and +1% health for all Blood Dominion units on the field per kill. There is no cap. In a large sustained battle, the Harvest makes The Sacrificed nearly unkillable through scale. The covenant is feeding.

The Hunger is active for the first 8 cycles of the Dark Doctrine Sacrificed's arrival, not 5. The unit does not accompany the manifestation. The manifestation intensifies because of the unit's presence.

---

### Faith Spread — Blood Dominion

Blood Dominion spreads through visibility. It does not require trade routes or cultural prestige. It requires that what it does be seen. A neighboring population with line of sight to a Blood Convocation does not experience abstract conversion pressure. They experience a specific encounter with a civilization that has something their own does not: a willingness to pay costs that makes it capable of things theirs cannot match. Some convert because they respect the power. Some because they fear becoming its material. Some because the theology genuinely answers something: that suffering has purpose, that obligation runs both ways between the strong and the structure they are sustained by.

Blood Dominion also spreads most aggressively in the immediate aftermath of conquest. Occupied territories experience maximum conversion pressure within the first 10 cycles — before the population has developed conceptual language to resist it. The covenant's physical presence in the territory is felt before the theology can be articulated, and by the time the theology can be articulated, the population has already been changed by witnessing the rituals.

### Conflict and Compatibility

**Blood Dominion vs. Old Light:** Old Light honors the dead by holding them in memory. Blood Dominion honors them by making the living use them as fuel. These are not reconcilable frameworks. Mixed populations experience constant pressure. In war, atrocity rates between these factions are elevated because each side interprets the other's ritual practice as active desecration of the dead and is therefore willing to commit acts beyond what military necessity requires.

**Blood Dominion vs. The Order:** The most functional cross-covenant relationship Blood Dominion has. The Order understands that structure requires cost, that hierarchy has obligations, that law is not sentiment. Two dark doctrine dynasties — Blood Rite and Order dark path — can cooperate because each recognizes in the other a theology that does not flinch from what power costs. The friction emerges from their competing accounts of what governs the covenant's relationship with ordinary people: Blood Dominion says sacrifice, The Order says compliance with law. These produce different populations in different states of subjugation, and eventually the two frameworks make claims the other cannot accommodate.

**Blood Dominion vs. The Wild:** Surprising structural compatibility at the theological level — both faiths accept the premise that the strong consume the weak as a basic fact of existence. Dark doctrine Blood Dominion and dark doctrine Wild can cooperate with minimal friction around this shared premise. The incompatibility emerges in practice: Blood Dominion wants to sacrifice nature as material (livestock, land, the labor of people living in it), and the Wild's dark doctrine holds that nature defines the terms of all consumption. What the Wild's dark path considers natural predation, Blood Dominion's dark path considers covenant sustenance, and eventually these descriptions make competing claims about the same piece of ground.

---

## THE ORDER — Covenant of the Sacred Mandate

### What This Faith Is

The Order does not hold that the divine speaks through fire, or through blood, or through the forest's indifferent growth. It holds that the divine speaks through structure. Through the fact that law exists, that hierarchy can be made to function, that human beings are capable of building institutions that outlast any individual and that constrain even those with the power to ignore them. In a world that nearly ended from chaos — from the collapse of every system that had organized people's lives — The Order offers the most specific answer to why The Great Frost came: because there was no law that held. Civilization failed not because it forgot the dead or refused to pay, but because it had no mechanism capable of making anyone accountable to anything larger than their immediate appetite. The Frost was the outcome of a world without binding law. The Mandate's answer is permanent, comprehensive, enforced law — not as convenience but as theology. Governance is not administration. It is the closest act to the sacred that living people can perform.

The Order asks its followers to believe something difficult: that the law matters more than the person. That an institution built correctly is more valuable than any individual within it, including the individual who built it. The light doctrine says this means the mandate exists to protect the governed — the law binds the powerful more than the weak, because the powerful have the most capacity to do harm unchecked. The dark doctrine says this means the mandate exists to enforce the hierarchy — the law defines who is governed and who governs, and compliance is the only expression of faith the institution requires. Both paths use the same texts. Both paths have enforced them at scale. The civilizations they produce are recognizably related and mutually incomprehensible.

### Light Doctrine — The Iron Compact

**The doctrine's logic:**

A dynasty walking the Iron Compact path has accepted that the law they invoke is the law they live under. The Mandate cannot be claimed as protection for the population while being used as a tool of extraction for the dynasty — the faith has no mechanism for this kind of hypocrisy that does not produce measurable structural consequences. The Iron Compact produces dynasties with unusual institutional stability because their populations experience governance as something other than organized exploitation. The law is the same for everyone, including the ruling bloodline, and the faith's rituals are specifically designed to enforce this through public ceremony that is genuinely binding rather than ceremonially hollow.

The vulnerability of this doctrine is bureaucratic scale. The Iron Compact operates through formal process — trials, declarations, oaths with legal weight, institutions that must be maintained. These require resources, personnel, and time that a crisis may not allow. An Iron Compact dynasty under rapid military pressure will find its governance machinery straining because the doctrine does not permit the summary shortcuts that crisis seems to demand. The dark path's appeal in moments of emergency is that it offers efficiency — one person deciding, no process required. The Iron Compact's answer is that efficiency purchased at the cost of the Mandate is not efficiency but dissolution of the thing that makes the dynasty worth defending.

**Rituals:**

**The Proclamation of Mandate**
- Building required: Lawpost (Tier 1) or higher
- Who participates: Senior law official as officiant; all Bloodline Members in the territory must attend (non-attendance is formally recorded and carries minor political consequence); population representatives from each settlement witness and acknowledge
- What is consumed: 20 Gold (for the official proclamation's material production), 15 Rare Materials (the formal documentation — these are legal instruments, not decorative objects), 20 Labor (for the process of drafting, reviewing, and formally witnessing). The Proclamation is a legal act. Its materials are the machinery of law.
- Duration: 1 cycle for the public ceremony, but requires 2 cycles of preparation.
- What happens to participants: The Bloodline Member who formally declares the Proclamation carries the Mandate Seal for 15 cycles — a status that makes all governance actions they take within this period 18% more effective. Population representatives who witness it return to their settlements carrying the Mandate Acknowledged status, which reduces their settlement's governance grievance accumulation by 20% for 10 cycles.
- Mechanical effect: All population settlements in the territory receive formal acknowledgment that the dynasty's authority derives from the Mandate rather than from force alone. Loyalty stability in all settlements increases for 12 cycles. Faith intensity +5%.

**The Trial of Account**
- Building required: Hall of Mandate (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Judge (a Bloodline Member or appointed specialist), defendant (a specific individual — a military commander, a settlement administrator, a Member of the bloodline who has taken actions that generated Conviction debt), witnesses from affected populations
- What is consumed: 30 Gold (for the formal court process), 25 Rare Materials (documentation), 25 Labor (the court's personnel). Most significantly: if the defendant is a Bloodline Member, their current administrative output is halted for the duration of the trial. The process costs real working time from actual people.
- Duration: 3 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The defendant, if found responsible, receives a formal Accounting — a permanent record in the Mandate that carries +15% Conviction accountability for their future decisions. This is not punitive in the conventional sense: it increases the weight of their choices. If acquitted, they carry the Cleared Record status — +10% effectiveness to all their actions for 15 cycles, affirmed by the Mandate.
- Mechanical effect: The Trial of Account formally resolves a specific governance grievance. Any outstanding population grievance tied to the defendant's administration is cleared upon the verdict. Faith intensity +7%. Note: a dynasty that consistently uses the Trial to protect powerful members from accountability will find that its population develops cynicism about the process — repeated acquittals of Bloodline Members while lower officials are convicted generates a Mandate Credibility erosion that compounds over cycles.
- Interruption: If the Hall of Mandate is attacked during the Trial, the proceedings are suspended. This generates a Mandate Violated event — faith intensity -5% and a governance crisis in the affected settlement.

**The Oath of Iron**
- Building required: Hall of Mandate (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: All Bloodline Members who will be bound by the oath — minimum 3, maximum the full bloodline; Mandate Officiant presides; population witnesses are required for the oath to carry its full legal weight
- What is consumed: 40 Gold, 30 Rare Materials (the formal oath documents, sealed with the dynasty's marks), 20 Provisions (the assembly requires feeding). Each participating Member formally binds themselves to a specific commitment — typically a policy regarding taxation, military service, or governance practice.
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: Members who take the oath carry the Iron Oath status for 30 cycles. While this status is active, they cannot take actions that contradict the oath's terms without generating a Mandate Breach event — which carries severe Conviction penalties and activates the faith's breach response. The oath is real. It constrains.
- Mechanical effect: Population trust in the dynasty increases significantly across all settlements covered by the oath's terms. Faith intensity +9%. The oath creates a formal accountability mechanism that functions even when political pressure would otherwise allow the dynasty to renege.
- Interruption: Cannot be interrupted. Oaths are not interrupted — they are kept or broken.

**The Grand Decree**
- Building required: The Iron Spire (Tier 3)
- Who participates: The full Iron Spire clergy; all Bloodline Members; population representatives from every settlement in the territory — the Grand Decree requires the widest possible witness to carry its full legal weight
- What is consumed: 65 Gold, 50 Rare Materials, 40 Provisions, 30 Faith Currency. The Decree is the Mandate's highest formal act — a permanent, irrevocable declaration with the full weight of the Iron Spire behind it.
- Duration: 1 cycle for the declaration; 3 cycles of preparation
- What happens to participants: The Bloodline Member who declares the Grand Decree is permanently marked as the Mandate's voice for this declaration — if they later violate its terms, the faith consequence is more severe than for any other member. This is not a distinction. It is a risk.
- Mechanical effect: The Grand Decree formally establishes a permanent governance framework affecting all territories under the dynasty's control. The specific terms of the Decree are player-chosen from a menu of governance options, each with different mechanical profiles — tax structures, military obligations, governance rights for various population tiers. The Decree's terms are binding and cannot be unilaterally changed. Changing them requires a Revocation Trial (another 3-cycle process). Faith intensity +14%.

**Passive doctrine effects:**

- Latent (0-20%): No doctrine effects.
- Active (20-40%): Governance infrastructure in all settlements generates 10% more administrative effectiveness — the faith provides a conceptual framework for why process matters that the population actively participates in rather than merely tolerating.
- Devout (40-60%): Rebellion probability in all settlements decreases 20% — populations governed by the Iron Compact understand that the law applies to the dynasty as well and that their grievances have a formal mechanism. This is not contentment. It is managed legitimacy.
- Fervent (60-80%): All Mandate-based governance actions (trials, decrees, proclamations) cost 20% fewer resources. The institutional machinery has reached a scale where it sustains itself. Enemy propaganda attempting to delegitimize the dynasty's authority fails at 40% higher rate — the population's experience of the Mandate as genuinely binding inoculates them against claims that the dynasty's rule is merely force dressed in legal language.
- Apex (80-100%): The Iron Legionary units become available with full Mandate enhancement. The dynasty's governance decisions carry cross-border weight: neutral factions begin treating Iron Compact decisions as legitimate precedent in diplomatic contexts, giving the dynasty unusual negotiating authority. Population conversion toward Order faith accelerates in territories where the dynasty's governance record is visible.
- Sustained Apex (10+ cycles): Rule of Law activates dynasty-wide. Every Bloodline Member gains immunity to external Conviction pressure as long as they remain in Mandate compliance. The governance record of the dynasty becomes a diplomatic asset with material mechanical weight.

**Spiritual manifestation — The Mandate's Presence (Fervent+):**

The Mandate's Presence manifests when The Order reaches Fervent intensity on the light doctrine path and a Grand Decree or Oath of Iron is currently active. It does not arrive dramatically. It settles. The Hall of Mandate or Iron Spire becomes the center of an atmospheric shift — the quality of sound changes, voices carry more clearly, ambient noise subsides. Documents and official seals glow with a faint gold-white illumination visible to everyone in the territory. Legal proceedings conducted during the Presence carry double weight. Oaths taken under it are permanent — they cannot be broken without triggering a catastrophic Conviction collapse rather than the normal breach penalty.

Mechanically, the Mandate's Presence creates a governance stabilization field across all territories it covers. All population loyalty effects move toward stability — grievances decay 30% faster, rebellions become 40% less likely, conversion away from Order faith slows to half rate. Iron Legionary and Iron Sentinel units within the affected territory gain +15% combat morale. Enemy units attempting to undermine governance infrastructure (raiding administrative buildings, conducting propaganda campaigns) find their success rate reduced by 35%. The Presence is not visible to enemies as a tactical entity. It is experienced as the absence of the vulnerabilities that governance normally creates.

Duration: 10 cycles. Ends if the Hall of Mandate or Iron Spire is destroyed, or if the active Decree or Oath that anchored the manifestation is breached. Breach of the anchoring document by any Bloodline Member immediately ends the Presence and generates a severe Mandate Violated event across all territories.

**Doctrine-specific unit abilities:**

**Iron Legionary (Level 3, Light Doctrine)**
The Iron Legionary is a soldier who has formally sworn their military service as a Mandate obligation rather than as employment or compulsion. This distinction is mechanical, not philosophical.
- Formation Discipline: When deployed in formation with 2 or more other Iron Legionaries, all units in the formation gain +15% damage resistance and +10% morale stability. The formation is the expression of the Mandate at the tactical level.
- Mandate's Witness: The Iron Legionary's presence on the field provides a passive Conviction accounting effect — any action the dynasty takes in territories where Iron Legionaries are deployed is assessed at its full Conviction weight rather than being moderated by distance or abstraction. They are the Mandate watching.
- Unyielding Post: Once per battle, the Iron Legionary may declare a position held — they cannot be moved, flanked, or forced back from this position for 5 cycles. They will die at this position if necessary, but the position will not be taken while they stand.

**Iron Sentinel (Level 4, Light Doctrine)**
The Iron Sentinel has risen through the formal meritocratic structure of The Order's military hierarchy — every promotion reviewed, documented, challenged if contested, confirmed by the institution.
- Command of Law: All units within 4 hexes of the Iron Sentinel gain the Mandate's Resolve passive — +12% morale and immunity to rout for 3 cycles after receiving orders from the Sentinel. The Sentinel's commands carry institutional weight.
- Formal Challenge: Once per battle, the Iron Sentinel may formally challenge an enemy unit or formation commander. The challenged unit must either accept direct combat (in which case the Sentinel gains +25% damage against it) or withdraw from the immediate engagement (in which case it forfeits its action for 2 cycles). This is not a psychological trick. It is a formal declaration with mechanical teeth.
- Iron Resolve: The Iron Sentinel cannot be affected by fear, corruption, or morale reduction from any source. Not because of personal courage but because the Mandate does not allow its enforcers to break.

**Apex unit — The Mandate (Light Doctrine expression):**
The Light Doctrine Mandate appears as a figure in full ceremonial-military armor — not parade armor but the kind worn at the formal execution of a sentence. Documents are visible, sealed with official marks. The unit moves through the battlefield as if conducting a formal proceeding, not a war — because in its theology, that is what it is doing.

Stats profile: High durability. Average offense compared to other apex units. The Mandate's offensive profile is not its power — its power is everything it does to the governance and morale environment around it. Governance grievances within its territory of operation decay at triple rate. Population morale becomes immune to military events while The Mandate is present — the institution is larger than the battle. Any enemy unit that kills The Mandate triggers a permanent Mandate Breach designation — they have violated the law — which gives all Order units that face them subsequently +20% damage against the specifically designated unit or formation.

Unique ability — The Judgment: Once per match, The Mandate formally adjudicates a territory. All enemy units in the territory are formally designated Non-Compliant and suffer -25% morale and -15% combat effectiveness for 10 cycles. All Order units in the territory gain +20% all stats. The territory itself is placed under active Mandate governance, making it immune to enemy faith conversion for 15 cycles. The Judgment has been issued. The territory has been heard.

---

### Dark Doctrine — The Iron Fist

**The doctrine's logic:**

The Iron Fist path takes the Iron Compact's theology and removes its condition. The Iron Compact holds that law binds the powerful as much as the weak, because the powerful have the most capacity for harm. The Iron Fist holds that this is sentimentality dressed as jurisprudence. The Mandate exists because some people are capable of enforcing order and some people require it to be enforced on them, and the law's function is not to balance these groups but to institutionalize their relationship. The dynasty is the Mandate. The Mandate is the dynasty. Compliance is the only legitimate expression of faith available to those who are not in the dynasty, because they are not in the position of interpreting the law — they are the population the law governs.

This doctrine produces the most administratively efficient civilizations in the game. The Iron Fist's institutional machinery does not slow down for consultation or formal process — it decides and enforces. Its population compliance rates are extremely high because non-compliance has been made formally illegal and the enforcement apparatus is comprehensive. What it produces in exchange for this efficiency is a civilization whose stability is entirely top-down — the population is compliant, not loyal, and the moment the dynasty demonstrates any weakness, the entire compliance architecture becomes available for capture by whoever can project authority over it.

**Rituals:**

**The Mandate Assertion**
- Building required: Lawpost (Tier 1) or higher
- Who participates: Warden-Enforcer units as the enforcement arm; law officials as formal declarants; the settlement's population as the audience — attendance is not optional
- What is consumed: 25 Gold (official apparatus), 20 Rare Materials (formal declaration documents), 20 Labor (enforcers and officials)
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: Warden-Enforcer units gain the Authority Mark for 8 cycles — visible status in portraits indicating active enforcement mandate. The settlement's population enters a Compliance State — reduced loyalty metric, replaced by a Compliance metric that functions differently. Compliance does not drift based on grievances the way loyalty does. It drops only when enforcement visibly fails.
- Mechanical effect: Faith intensity in the settlement +7%. Compliance replaces loyalty in the settlement for 20 cycles. During this period, rebellion probability is extremely low (compliance threshold must be specifically broken rather than gradually drifting). However, if any enforcement action fails publicly in this settlement, the Compliance metric drops catastrophically rather than gradually. Faith intensity +7%.

**The Formal Supression**
- Building required: Hall of Mandate (Tier 2) or higher, Warden-Enforcer unit present
- Who participates: Warden-Enforcer as primary enforcement agent; law officials as documentation apparatus; the targeted population as material
- What is consumed: 30 Gold, 25 Rare Materials, 30 Labor. The targeted population segment — those designated as Non-Compliant — are formally processed: fined, relocated, imprisoned in the game's abstraction, or eliminated depending on Conviction level of the ruling dynasty.
- Duration: 3 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Warden-Enforcer conducting the suppression gains +15% combat effectiveness for 10 cycles — they have been formally affirmed in their function. The settlement's remaining population experiences a Compliance Reset — all compliance metrics return to baseline and the memory of the suppression functions as a deterrent for 20 cycles.
- Mechanical effect: All non-Order faith activity in the targeted settlement is eliminated for 20 cycles. Enemy faith conversion pressure is zeroed out for the same period. Faith intensity +9%. Adjacent settlements with line of sight generate a Compliance Response — some will adopt compliance preemptively to avoid being the next target. This is a mechanical effect, not a narrative description.

**The Mandate's Decree**
- Building required: Hall of Mandate (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Dynasty-level law officials; all Bloodline Members must ratify; the Decree is then enforced by Warden-Enforcer units in every settlement within reach
- What is consumed: 45 Gold, 35 Rare Materials, 15 Faith Currency. The Decree is a formal governance act with enforcement apparatus built into it.
- Duration: 2 cycles to declare; effects are permanent unless actively revoked.
- What happens to participants: All Bloodline Members who ratify gain +10% to all command actions for 20 cycles — the formal exercise of authority generates its own return. The dynasty's governance machinery becomes more efficient as the Decree rationalizes administrative procedure.
- Mechanical effect: Formally establishes a governance framework with enforcement teeth. Player chooses from menu of Iron Fist decree types, each with different mechanics — taxation maximization, military conscription increases, religious compliance enforcement, administrative centralization. All are effective. All reduce population happiness while increasing compliance. Faith intensity +8%.

**The Iron Judgment**
- Building required: The Iron Spire (Tier 3)
- Who participates: The Iron Spire's full institutional apparatus; Warden-Enforcer and Dominion Arbiter units as enforcement; a Bloodline Member must personally deliver the Judgment; the defendant territory or population has no procedural role
- What is consumed: 70 Gold, 60 Rare Materials, 50 Labor, 35 Faith Currency. The Iron Judgment is the most resource-intensive act in The Order's dark doctrine toolkit.
- Duration: 1 cycle for delivery, 4 cycles of preparation by the institution
- What happens to participants: The Bloodline Member who delivers the Judgment carries the Arbiter's Mark permanently for this match — they are the Mandate's final word. All enforcement actions they subsequently order carry +25% institutional weight, meaning enforcement success rates are higher and compliance responses from adjacent settlements are more immediate.
- Mechanical effect: A specific territory or enemy dynasty is formally placed under Iron Judgment — a permanent designation with specific enforcement mechanics. All Order units fighting a dynasty under Iron Judgment gain +25% combat morale for 25 cycles. Neutral factions experience -15% trade willingness toward the judged entity. The Iron Spire's institutional network generates passive intelligence on the judged entity's military movements (the law has declared them Non-Compliant; it watches them accordingly). Faith intensity +15%.

**Passive doctrine effects:**

- Latent (0-20%): No doctrine effects.
- Active (20-40%): Administrative governance actions process 12% faster — the doctrine removes the consultative delays the Iron Compact considers essential. Warden-Enforcer units become available.
- Devout (40-60%): Compliance replaces loyalty as the dominant population state across all home territories. This produces very stable governance metrics but eliminates the morale bonuses that loyalty provides. Populations under compliance do not generate faith intensity spontaneously — it must be supplied top-down rather than emerging organically.
- Fervent (60-80%): All administrative and enforcement actions cost 25% fewer resources. Populations in adjacent territories experience conversion pressure toward The Order not through attraction but through observation of the dynasty's institutional coherence. Neighbors understand that this civilization will not collapse from internal disorder, and some convert to align with the likely future dominant power. This is a rational mechanics.
- Apex (80-100%): Total administrative saturation. Every settlement's governance metrics are tracked in real time and accessible as intelligence. Governance failures are detected 5 cycles before they become crises. Dominion Arbiter units gain their full combat package. The Iron Judgment can now be issued against multiple targets simultaneously.
- Sustained Apex (10+ cycles): The Long Arm activates. The dynasty's enforcement authority extends into adjacent territories not under its governance control — enemy populations within 3 hexes of Order territory begin experiencing Mandate compliance pressure, gradually converting governance loyalty into cross-border compliance with The Order's institutional framework. This is slow and not inevitable, but it is real.

**Spiritual manifestation — The Mandate's Weight (Fervent+):**

Where the Iron Compact's manifestation is a settling — a presence that stabilizes and witnesses — the Iron Fist's manifestation is a pressure. The Mandate's Weight arrives as an atmospheric phenomenon centered on the Iron Spire: the air becomes heavier, sounds become flatter, movements require slightly more effort to initiate. This is not visible the way fire or a spectral presence is visible. It is felt. Every non-Order unit in the territory experiences it as a persistent mechanical penalty — -8% to all stats for the manifestation's duration, from the sheer weight of institutional authority at its most total expression. Order units experience it as clarity — +12% all stats for the same duration.

The Mandate's Weight also has a specific enforcement function: any unit designated Non-Compliant (by any Order ritual or decree) that operates within the affected territory suffers double the standard designation penalties. The institutional designation and the spiritual pressure reinforce each other. Governance grievances within the territory do not accumulate during the manifestation — the weight of the institution suppresses them.

Duration: 10 cycles. Ends when the Iron Spire is destroyed or faith intensity drops below Fervent. Cannot be directly attacked. Counter-ritual from a Fervent or higher Blood Dominion or Wild faith can erode it over 3 cycles.

**Doctrine-specific unit abilities:**

**Warden-Enforcer (Level 3, Dark Doctrine)**
The Warden-Enforcer is not a soldier in the conventional sense. They are the Mandate's enforcement arm operating in territories where the law is being actively contested.
- Authority Presence: All units within 3 hexes of the Warden-Enforcer gain +10% morale stability and -5% rout probability. The institutional authority the Warden carries functions as morale infrastructure.
- Compliance Enforcement: Once per battle, the Warden-Enforcer may formally designate an enemy unit as Non-Compliant. That unit suffers -18% all stats for 5 cycles and becomes a priority target marked for all Order units.
- Suppression Line: The Warden-Enforcer can establish a formal suppression cordon around a specific 4-hex area. Non-Order units attempting to enter or exit this area suffer +20% damage during transit for 3 cycles. The cordon is a declaration of Order's enforcement authority, not merely a tactical formation.

**Dominion Arbiter (Level 4, Dark Doctrine)**
The Dominion Arbiter is the Iron Fist's judicial-military hybrid — an officer with both enforcement authority and the institutional mandate to pass binding decisions in the field without referral to higher governance.
- Binding Judgment: Once per battle, the Arbiter issues a formal judgment against a specific enemy unit or formation. That unit or formation is Condemned — suffering -20% all stats permanently for the duration of the match (not just the battle). If the condemned unit or formation is killed, the death generates +5% faith intensity directly.
- Arbiter's Authority: All Order units within 5 hexes of the Arbiter gain immunity to enemy morale manipulation and propaganda effects. The Mandate's formal authority is more credible than any enemy's informal persuasion.
- Iron Sentence: The Arbiter can end any non-Order unit's rout state by targeting them — they are formally compelled to stop fleeing and face the Mandate's enforcement. The targeted unit's rout ends, but they fight at -15% stats for 3 cycles from the psychological weight of being formally compelled.

**Apex unit — The Mandate (Dark Doctrine expression):**
The Dark Doctrine Mandate does not carry documents. It carries a formal seal and a drawn blade. Where the light version walks through the battle as if conducting a proceeding, the dark version walks through it as if delivering a sentence that was decided before the battle began. The armor is the same ceremonial-military design, but nothing about the figure's posture suggests the outcome is in question. The sentence was issued. Its enforcement is a formality.

Stats profile: Higher base damage than the light doctrine version — +25%. The same governance-stabilizing passive effects, but with Compliance rather than loyalty as the target metric. Where the light doctrine Mandate increases genuine population investment in Order governance, the dark doctrine Mandate maximizes compliance-through-authority. Governance grievances cannot accumulate in any territory The Mandate occupies — they are suppressed rather than resolved, which is faster and more fragile.

Unique ability — The Final Sentence: Once per match, The Mandate issues a formal order dissolving the enemy's institutional claims to legitimacy in the territory. All enemy units in the affected territory lose any morale bonus derived from conviction, faith, or dynastic identity for 15 cycles — they are fighting, but the Mandate has formally declared them to be without legitimate standing. Order units in the same territory gain +25% all stats for the same duration. The psychological weight of institutional delegitimization applied at scale.

---

### Faith Spread — The Order

The Order spreads through adjacent populations observing institutional stability over time. It does not spread through conquest primarily — it spreads through the contrast between the dynasty's governance coherence and the disorder elsewhere. Populations experiencing factional conflict, governance failure, or arbitrary rule are most susceptible to Order conversion because the faith offers the most concrete answer to disorder: law, hierarchy, enforcement. Populations in neighboring territories who have watched an Order dynasty for 10 cycles and found it consistently more stable than their own governance begin converting not out of idealism but out of pragmatic calculation.

Dark doctrine Order spreads through a different mechanism: it spreads through the demonstration that non-compliance has visible, predictable costs. A Formal Suppression conducted in line of sight of neighboring territories generates conversion pressure in those territories not because the observers find the doctrine attractive but because the alternative to converting has been made observable.

### Conflict and Compatibility

**The Order vs. Old Light:** Structurally compatible until tested by specific circumstances. The Record and the Law are not incompatible as foundations for a civilization. The fracture emerges around the question of whether accountability processes are genuine or procedural — Old Light demands emotional and moral acknowledgment of cost, The Order demands formal procedural compliance. These produce very different governance cultures when mercy, exceptions, and the treatment of conquered populations become active questions.

**The Order vs. Blood Dominion:** The most functional dark-doctrine cross-covenant pairing. Both accept that power requires cost, that hierarchy has obligations, that law and sacrifice are mechanisms for maintaining what would otherwise dissolve. Cooperation is achievable between two dark-doctrine dynasties of these faiths because neither will try to moralize the other out of their methods. The conflict emerges when their frameworks make competing claims about the same territory: Blood Dominion wants to sacrifice it, The Order wants to govern it. These are not reconcilable at scale.

**The Order vs. The Wild:** The deepest structural incompatibility in the game, more fundamental even than Old Light vs. The Wild. The Order holds that civilization and its laws are the highest achievement available to human beings. The Wild holds that civilization is a temporary arrangement that the world tolerates conditionally and will eventually end. The Order's entire theology is a refutation of the Wild's, and vice versa. Two dynasties of these faiths cannot coexist at high faith intensity without one actively working to eliminate the other's cultural presence.

---

## THE WILD — Covenant of the Thorned Elder Roots

### What This Faith Is

The Wild does not hold that the divine is memory, or covenant, or law. It holds that the divine is the fact of the world — the indifferent, total, prior-to-everything fact of existence before human beings arrived and began naming things and building structures and deciding that their arrangements were important. The Great Frost was not punishment, not consequence, not abandonment. It was the world asserting what it always was: larger than what had been built inside it, indifferent to the buildings, patient in the way that things are patient when they do not die. The civilizations that fell to the Frost were not being punished. They were being reminded. The Wild's theology is built on this reminder, and it is the most ancient faith in the game precisely because it requires the least from the world to remain true. The forest does not need to believe in The Wild for The Wild's theology to be correct.

The Wild makes an offer to its followers that no other faith makes: it does not promise that your civilization will survive. It does not promise that what you build will last. It promises that if you understand your relationship to the world correctly — if you acknowledge what you are and what the world is, if you take what the world provides without pretending you own it, if you contribute to the living system you exist within rather than simply extracting from it — then you will be part of something larger than your dynasty, and that something will outlast you even when you do not. The light doctrine builds toward a kind of sacred participation in the world's life. The dark doctrine concludes that participation is for the weak, that natural law is predation, and that winter is not cruel because cruelty requires intention and winter has none.

### Light Doctrine — The Living Compact

**The doctrine's logic:**

A dynasty walking the Living Compact path has accepted that the world is a partner rather than a resource. Its territories are managed the way a person manages a relationship — you take, and you give back, and the accounting is not purely financial. The faith's rituals are structured as exchanges: you release something into the natural world, and the world responds. You take something from what it provides, and you acknowledge the taking formally. You mark territory not with walls but with living things — planted groves, maintained waterways, designated resting territories for animals. These acts are not conservation in the modern ecological sense. They are covenant-keeping in the oldest sense: the dynasty has made an agreement with the ground it stands on, and the ground is aware of the agreement.

This doctrine's military and strategic vulnerability is its geography-dependence. The Living Compact's strongest bonuses are generated within territories the dynasty has genuinely maintained over time — territories with active Grove Markers, Spirit Lodges functioning at full capacity, Thornrider units that have been stationed in the territory long enough to know its patterns. A Living Compact dynasty that has been in a territory for 20 cycles fights very differently in that territory than a dynasty that arrived 5 cycles ago. This is intentional. The faith rewards depth of presence over breadth of conquest.

**Rituals:**

**The Grove-Binding**
- Building required: Grove Marker (Tier 1) or higher
- Who participates: Thornrider unit as primary officiant (the faith's relationship with mounted cavalry is ancient and the Thornrider is both military unit and sacred figure); any Bloodline Member with the Naturalist trait if available; the participating population provides labor and materials
- What is consumed: 20 Timber drawn from managed sustainable harvest (a specific resource category distinct from clear-cut timber), 15 Provisions (to be left at the grove site for the animals that will use it), 10 Rare Materials (living materials — seeds, cultivated root structures, collected sacred stones). A portion of the dynasty's food production is designated as Grove Tithe for 8 cycles following the rite.
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Thornrider officiant develops a Grove Bond with the territory — they fight at +12% effectiveness within this territory for 20 cycles and their mounted unit does not suffer movement penalties in the territory's terrain type regardless of weather. The Bloodline Member who participated gains the Grove-Bound trait for 15 cycles, making them immune to Conviction penalties from natural events (droughts, floods, animal attacks) in this territory — they are understood to be in relationship with the forces generating these events.
- Mechanical effect: The territory gains a Living Compact designation — all Wild faith intensity generation in the territory increases 18% for as long as the Grove is maintained. Territory defense bonuses increase 12% as the dynasty has declared partnership with the terrain. Faith intensity +6%.

**The Release**
- Building required: Spirit Lodge (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Spirit Lodge elder as officiant; a Bloodline Member who will conduct the release; a captured or consecrated living thing — an animal from the dynasty's herds, or a captured enemy unit, depending on Conviction level; the presence of Thornrider or Bear Rider units is traditional though not required
- What is consumed: What is released is what is consumed. For the light doctrine path: the release is an animal — a healthy animal from the dynasty's best breeding stock, released into the wild unmarked and unretained. 30 Provisions (the animal's preparation and the ceremony's sustenance), 20 Rare Materials (sacred markings applied to the released animal, made from natural materials). The loss of a prime animal is a real resource cost.
- Duration: 1 cycle.
- What happens to participants: The Bloodline Member who performs the release gains the Released One trait for 15 cycles — they experience the world differently following the act of unconditional giving, which the faith understands as opening perception. This trait gives them +20% to any Conviction check involving restraint — not attacking when attacking would benefit them, not taking when taking would profit them. It reinforces the capacity for the action most difficult within a strategy game's logic.
- Mechanical effect: The Wild's spirit presence in the territory intensifies for 15 cycles. Scouting actions by enemy forces in the territory fail at 25% higher rate — the land's awareness of what passes through it has increased. All Grove-Bound units in the territory gain a passive regeneration of 3% health per cycle. Faith intensity +8%.

**The Thornrider's Claim**
- Building required: Spirit Lodge (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Thornrider unit leads; the dynasty's mounted cavalry must be present and formally brought through the territory; the route covers every major terrain feature of the claimed territory
- What is consumed: 35 Provisions (for the journey and the animals), 25 Rare Materials (territory markers — living materials planted along the route), 15 Gold (for the formal documentation of the claim). The Thornrider unit is unavailable for combat during the 3 cycles of the claim journey.
- Duration: 3 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Thornrider unit that conducts the claim gains the Territory Knowing trait for the territory permanently — +15% combat effectiveness within this specific territory for the remainder of the match, and +8% effectiveness in all territories of the same terrain type encountered subsequently.
- Mechanical effect: The claimed territory is formally recognized within the Wild's covenant as belonging to this dynasty's stewardship. All enemy units entering the territory trigger a detection event 2 cycles earlier than normal — the land has been told who belongs here. Territory defense bonuses increase an additional 15%. Faith intensity +9%.

**The First Grove**
- Building required: The First Grove (Tier 3)
- Who participates: Full First Grove clergy; all Bloodline Members present in the territory; the most senior Thornrider and Bear Rider units available; population representatives from every settlement
- What is consumed: 70 Provisions (the First Grove ceremony is an extended communal event), 55 Timber from managed harvest, 45 Rare Materials (living materials contributed from across the dynasty's territories), 35 Faith Currency. The First Grove ceremony requires that no military action be initiated by the dynasty for its 5-cycle duration — the covenant cannot be established while the dynasty is actively at war.
- Duration: 5 cycles, during which the dynasty is formally in covenant ceremony and cannot initiate combat (defensive response is permitted; new offensive actions are not).
- What happens to participants: All Bloodline Members gain the Grove Covenant trait permanently for this match — they fight at +10% effectiveness in any territory with an established Grove Marker, and their decisions in governance and diplomacy carry the Wild's formal endorsement weight (neutral factions treat their overtures at +20% effectiveness).
- Mechanical effect: Establishes a dynasty-wide covenant designation with the Wild. All territories with Grove Markers receive a permanent +15% bonus to faith intensity generation. The First Grove building becomes the center of the dynasty's spiritual presence on the map — all spirit manifestations originate from it. Faith intensity +18%.

**Passive doctrine effects:**

- Latent (0-20%): No doctrine effects.
- Active (20-40%): Territory traversal by enemy scouting units is 10% more likely to be detected. The dynasty's own units move 8% faster through natural terrain. The faith has begun to align the dynasty with the territory's patterns.
- Devout (40-60%): All weather events in home territory generate positive effects rather than negative — rain improves food production, frost strengthens defensive terrain, storms reduce enemy visibility. The dynasty has been in relationship with these patterns long enough that they cooperate. This requires continuous Grove maintenance; if Groves go unattended for 5 cycles, this benefit begins to degrade.
- Fervent (60-80%): Thornrider and Bear Rider units gain Territory Knowing passively in any territory where a Grove Marker has been active for 10+ cycles. All Grove-Bound units regenerate health 25% faster in home territory. The Release rites cost 20% fewer resources.
- Apex (80-100%): The dynasty's territories have achieved full Living Compact designation. Terrain in home territory generates active defensive effects — forest cover improves beyond the standard defensive bonus, waterways create additional movement penalties for non-Wild units, elevation advantages increase. The territory itself is participating in defense. Additionally, Wild faith spread through adjacent territories accelerates: the health and productivity of the dynasty's territory is visible to neighboring populations as evidence of what the faith produces.
- Sustained Apex (10+ cycles): The Breathing Land activates. Home territory generates 1% faith intensity per cycle passively without any investment — the covenant has reached a state of self-sustenance. Population in home territory does not experience resource scarcity penalties during seasonal events because the covenant has formally aligned the dynasty's resource cycle with the territory's production cycle.

**Spiritual manifestation — The Waking Green (Fervent+):**

The Waking Green manifests when The Wild reaches Fervent intensity on the light doctrine path and at least two Grove Markers have been actively maintained in the territory for 15 or more cycles. What arrives is ecological, not supernatural in any conventional sense. Vegetation in the territory appears to accelerate — vines move visibly along defensive structures, roots appear along paths where there were no roots, animal presence intensifies. This is not dramatic. It is steady and comprehensive, the way a forest demonstrates its indifference to what happens at the scale of a battle.

Mechanically, the Waking Green activates all terrain bonuses in the territory at 150% normal value. Forest cover grants +30% rather than +20% defensive bonus. Elevated terrain increases movement cost for non-Wild units by an additional 15%. Any enemy unit that enters dense natural terrain in the territory suffers a 3-hex vision reduction that lasts until they exit the terrain. Wild units — Thornriders, Bear Riders, Forest Sovereigns — gain complete immunity to terrain movement penalties for the duration.

Duration: 12 cycles. Ends if both anchor Grove Markers are destroyed or faith intensity drops below Fervent. The manifestation cannot be directly attacked. Enemy forces can end it by destroying the Grove Markers, which requires finding them first.

**Doctrine-specific unit abilities:**

**Thornrider (Level 3, Light Doctrine)**
The Thornrider is the living expression of the Living Compact's covenant with motion — a mounted unit that exists in continuous relationship with the territory it moves through, not as a traveler but as a recognized presence.
- Territory Reading: In any territory where the dynasty has an active Grove Marker, the Thornrider gains +15% all stats. This is not a bonus granted by the building. It is the building's presence indicating that the Thornrider belongs there.
- The Quiet Approach: Thornriders in natural terrain do not trigger enemy detection until they are within 2 hexes of the detecting unit, regardless of the detecting unit's normal detection radius. They move with the patterns of the territory rather than against them.
- Grove Bonding: When a Thornrider is stationed in a territory for 3 consecutive cycles, they establish a passive bond with that territory. If attacked in their bonded territory, they gain a one-time +20% combat effectiveness surge — the territory's awareness of the attack is shared with the rider.

**Forest Sovereign (Level 4, Light Doctrine)**
The Forest Sovereign has spent enough time in the covenant relationship that the relationship has changed them. Their connection to territory is not a tactical bonus. It is something else.
- Living Armor: The Forest Sovereign's defense increases by 1% per cycle they spend in natural terrain, up to a maximum of 20%. This accumulation resets if they leave natural terrain for 3 cycles. The covenant of sustained presence produces sustained return.
- Sovereign Sight: Within any territory designated as the dynasty's Living Compact territory, the Forest Sovereign can see all units regardless of fog of war. The territory knows what passes through it and shares what it knows.
- Grove Call: Once per battle, the Forest Sovereign calls the territory's cooperation. For 3 cycles, all terrain bonuses in the battle's location are calculated at 200% normal value. Enemy units in terrain penalty states during this period suffer double the standard penalty. The territory has been formally requested to assist, and it has agreed.

**Apex unit — The First Wild (Light Doctrine expression):**
The Light Doctrine First Wild appears as something between a rider and a grove — the figure is mounted, armored, but the armor has become partially incorporated with natural material, branches and living growth wound through the metal, the mount an animal so old that the distinction between wild and tamed has ceased to be meaningful. This unit looks like what happens to a person when they have honored the covenant long enough that the covenant has begun to honor them back.

Stats profile: Moderate offense, high mobility, very high terrain advantage multipliers. The First Wild does not fight best in open field. It fights best where the dynasty has been. In a territory with 20+ cycles of Grove presence, The First Wild's combat effectiveness is 45% higher than its base stats — the living land amplifies it. Out of such territory, it is a capable apex unit. In its home ground, it is nearly unbeatable.

Unique ability — The Grove's Memory: Once per match, The First Wild invokes the full memory of the dynasty's covenant relationship with all territory it has ever maintained a Grove in. For 10 cycles, all allied units in any Living Compact territory gain the Breathing Land's full defensive benefits regardless of whether they are in natural terrain. The territory everywhere the dynasty has tended is active simultaneously. The Waking Green manifests in all of these territories simultaneously for the duration.

---

### Dark Doctrine — The Consuming Wild

**The doctrine's logic:**

The Consuming Wild has heard the Living Compact's theology and decided that half of it is sentiment. The world is indifferent. The Wild's theology is built on that truth. The Consuming Wild path takes that truth to its complete form: if the world is indifferent, and if indifference is the law, and if winter kills the weak and strengthens the herd, then mercy is not a virtue — it is a refusal to participate honestly in what the world is. The forest does not preserve the lame deer. The flood does not spare the poorly positioned. The strong consume the weak because that is the mechanism through which the living world maintains itself, and a civilization that replicates this mechanism is not cruel. It is accurate.

This doctrine produces dynasties of genuine military ferocity — the Consuming Wild is the most aggressive faith path in the game in its territory control mechanics, because the faith frames expansion and predation as theologically correct rather than as moral compromises. What it produces in exchange for this ferocity is a civilization with no mechanism for the kind of internal solidarity that other faiths generate. The Consuming Wild population is not bonded by covenant, or law, or shared memory. It is bonded by strength. The moment the dynasty shows weakness, the binding unravels.

**Rituals:**

**The Culling**
- Building required: Grove Marker (Tier 1) or higher, Bear Rider unit present
- Who participates: Bear Rider oversees; the designated population — the dynasty's weakest settlement, determined by lowest productivity and loyalty metrics, is selected — contributes its least productive members as the ritual material; no ceremony beyond the act itself
- What is consumed: The designated individuals. The settlement's population decreases. 15 Rare Materials (the markers placed to formally designate the cull zone), 20 Gold (for the Bear Rider unit's deployment and logistics). The dynasty's food production improves because the population consuming resources has been reduced.
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Bear Rider unit carries the Culling Mark for 10 cycles — all combat effectiveness +18%. The remaining population in the culled settlement enters a Survival Hierarchy state: loyalty is replaced by a dominance compliance metric that measures whether the population believes the dynasty is the strongest available authority. It will hold as long as the dynasty is winning.
- Mechanical effect: Food production improves 8% in the territory as resource pressure decreases. Faith intensity +7%. All military units in the territory gain +12% combat morale for 10 cycles. The world has been shown that this dynasty understands what the world is.

**The Pack Rite**
- Building required: Spirit Lodge (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Senior Consuming Wild priest; all combat units currently stationed in the territory; the prey — captive enemy soldiers or designated local population as material; Bear Rider or Bonebreaker unit leads the rite
- What is consumed: 35 Provisions, 25 Rare Materials (sacred predator markings applied to participating units), 20 Gold. The prey is consumed within the rite — not symbolically.
- Duration: 2 cycles.
- What happens to participants: All participating combat units gain the Pack-Marked status for 12 cycles — +15% damage, +10% movement speed, and the ability to coordinate attacks without movement penalty (the pack moves as one organism). The priest who conducts the rite gains the Apex status for 8 cycles — their faith intensity generation doubles.
- Mechanical effect: Faith intensity +10%. All Pack-Marked units function as a coordinated formation even when geographically dispersed — they share a morale pool during Pack-Marked duration rather than individual morale meters. This makes them resistant to morale manipulation (you cannot break half the pack's morale without breaking all of it) but vulnerable to a single concentrated morale collapse (if the pack breaks, all Pack-Marked units rout simultaneously).

**The Territory Claim**
- Building required: Spirit Lodge (Tier 2) or higher
- Who participates: Bonebreaker unit leads; the dynasty's military force physically occupies the territory; population of the occupied territory is not consulted
- What is consumed: 40 Gold, 30 Rare Materials (Consuming Wild territory markers — these are predator markers, not living materials), 25 Labor. The Bonebreaker unit is engaged in the territory for the duration.
- Duration: 3 cycles.
- What happens to participants: The Bonebreaker unit carries the Apex Claim for the territory permanently — +15% combat effectiveness in this specific territory for the remainder of the match. Any enemy unit entering the territory triggers an automatic detection event immediately (the markers have saturated the territory's awareness with a different signal than the Living Compact's gentle detection).
- Mechanical effect: The territory is formally claimed under the Consuming Wild's authority. Enemy populations in the territory immediately begin faith conversion toward the Wild at +20% rate — not through attraction but through the understanding that the alternative is being the next subject of the Culling. Faith intensity +9%.

**The Apex Feast**
- Building required: The First Grove (Tier 3)
- Who participates: Full Consuming Wild clergy; all apex predator units (Bear Riders, Bonebreakers) present in the territory; a mass of captives serves as the ritual's material; a Bloodline Member must preside
- What is consumed: 75 Provisions, 55 Rare Materials, 45 Gold, 35 Faith Currency. And the captives. The Apex Feast is the Consuming Wild's most definitive ritual statement.
- Duration: 4 cycles, during which the territory generates Horror events visible to all neighboring factions.
- What happens to participants: All Bonebreaker and Bear Rider units in the territory gain the Feast-Blessed status for 25 cycles — +20% all combat stats, immunity to morale reduction from any source, health regeneration 5% per cycle during combat. The Bloodline Member who presides carries the Apex Mark permanently for this match.
- Mechanical effect: The territory's faith intensity reaches maximum possible value for 15 cycles. All Wild units across the entire dynasty gain +10% combat effectiveness for 15 cycles — the Feast's spiritual impact radiates outward. Horror events generated reach 16 hexes, affecting all neighbors simultaneously. Faith intensity +20%.

**Passive doctrine effects:**

- Latent (0-20%): No doctrine effects.
- Active (20-40%): Combat units gain +5% damage when fighting in natural terrain. The Culling option becomes available. Bear Rider units are available.
- Devout (40-60%): Enemy populations within 2 hexes of Consuming Wild territory begin experiencing conversion pressure not through attraction but through proximity to something that has demonstrated it can consume them. The conversion rate is slow but persistent. Capturing enemy units generates partial faith intensity as a passive — the covenant is fed by predatory success.
- Fervent (60-80%): Pack Rite costs 25% fewer resources. All combat units gain +8% movement speed in natural terrain. Routing enemy units within Consuming Wild territory are automatically hunted by any available Wild unit without command input — the hunting instinct has been mechanically formalized.
- Apex (80-100%): Territory claimed under the Consuming Wild generates passive predation pressure on all adjacent unclaimed territory — enemy resource production within 3 hexes of Consuming Wild territory decreases 5% per cycle as the covenant's presence diminishes what the adjacent land is willing to provide for those not part of the compact. All Bonebreaker units gain their full combat package permanently.
- Sustained Apex (10+ cycles): The Feeding Ground activates. Every military engagement victory in home territory generates an automatic faith intensity bonus of 2% — the land has registered the kill and fed on it. The distinction between war and ritual has been collapsed.

**Spiritual manifestation — The Cold (Fervent+):**

The Cold does not announce itself. It arrives as a temperature drop in the affected territory — perceptible, consistent, and deepening over the first 3 cycles following manifestation. There is no visual entity. There is no sound. There is winter, in a localized and immediate form, and the territory has gone still the way territory goes still before predation.

Mechanically, The Cold imposes a cold-weather penalty on all non-Wild units in the affected territory — -8% movement speed, -5% morale, and +10% resource consumption for food (the units are cold, and cold bodies require more to maintain combat effectiveness). Wild units are immune to all Cold effects. The Cold persists as a territorial condition for 15 cycles and generates a passive secondary effect: enemy units that spend 5 or more cycles within The Cold without being engaged in combat suffer a stack of Winter-Touched, reducing their maximum health ceiling 5% per stack. The Wild did not create this. It invited it. The Frost was always there.

Duration: 15 cycles. Unlike most manifestations, The Cold cannot be ended by destroying a building — the cold does not live in a building. It can only be countered by a sustained Old Light Great Blessing or a Blood Dominion Grand Rite of sufficient scale, which produce enough warmth and violence respectively to displace it. Counter-ritual effort from either faith takes 5 cycles to complete.

**Doctrine-specific unit abilities:**

**Bonebreaker (Level 3, Dark Doctrine)**
The Bonebreaker is the Consuming Wild's predator specialist — a rider trained specifically to isolate, pursue, and eliminate the weakest element of any opposing force.
- Predator Sight: At the start of any engagement, the Bonebreaker automatically identifies the enemy unit with the lowest current health percentage in the area. This unit is marked as prey — the Bonebreaker deals +25% damage to it specifically.
- Pursuit Mastery: When an enemy unit routs, the Bonebreaker gains +30% movement speed for 3 cycles and prioritizes pursuit automatically. Routed units cannot escape the Bonebreaker through movement alone.
- The Culling Blow: Once per battle, the Bonebreaker performs a targeted kill attack on a unit below 20% health, bypassing normal combat resolution. The targeted unit is eliminated without a combat roll. This can be used on any unit below the threshold, including Bloodline Members if they are present on the field and meet the condition.

**Bear Rider (Level 4, Dark Doctrine)**
The Bear Rider is the Consuming Wild's apex mounted unit — a rider bonded to a bear through the Consuming Wild's animal covenant, which is different from the Living Compact's gentle grove-bonding in every way that matters.
- Pack Alpha: All Bonebreaker units within 5 hexes of the Bear Rider gain +15% combat effectiveness. They are the pack. The Bear Rider is what they orient toward.
- Crushing Advance: The Bear Rider's charge attack ignores the target's defensive terrain bonus entirely — mountains, forest, fortifications. The bear does not recognize human arrangements as obstacles.
